previous_plan.title: Smart Building AI Intrusion Detection System v2
previous_plan.executive_summary: Real-time transformer-based anomaly detection over access events and sensor triggers on AWS ECS with async LLM narrative generation. Round 2 added pseudonymization before LLM calls and blue-green model deployment.
previous_plan.architecture_overview: Four ECS services: event-ingestion, inference-service (GPU task), alert-service with WebSocket, narrative-service (async LLM). TimescaleDB for event time-series, Redis for real-time alert state. Keycloak OIDC with MFA.
previous_plan.security_and_compliance: Keycloak RBAC, mTLS between services, pseudonymization before LLM. GDPR erasure deletes access_events but corrupts model labels.
previous_plan.phased_implementation: Phase 1 rule-based MVP. Phase 2 transformer model. Phase 3 LLM narratives. Fine-tuning CronJob weekly without regression gate.

target_output.thinking_summary: Round 3 closes two remaining issues: ISS-002 requires adding a held-out labeled validation set (20% split) as a regression gate in the fine-tuning CronJob before blue-green model promotion, with automatic rollback if recall drops below threshold; ISS-003 requires replacing DELETE on training labels with pseudonym token rotation using a new HMAC salt, severing person-to-label linkage without removing training records. Both fixes are surgical additions to the existing architecture which is otherwise sound at round 2.

fix_report.action_taken: Added model regression gate to the weekly fine-tuning CronJob: 20% of labeled events held out as validation set; fine-tuned model must achieve recall >= previous model recall - 0.02 and precision >= 0.75 on held-out set before GitLab CI promotes the blue-green ECS task replacement. Automatic rollback triggered if smoke test fails post-promotion.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Model quality degradation from labeling bias in a single week is caught before production deployment; maintained detection recall on held-out benchmark set.

fix_report.action_taken: Redesigned GDPR erasure cascade: instead of deleting training label records for a person, the erasure job re-computes the pseudonym token with a new HMAC rotation key for that person, updates all model_training_labels records with the new pseudonym, and deletes the original person_id-to-pseudonym mapping from the pseudonym_registry. This severs the linkage while preserving training data integrity.
fix_report.expected_outcome: GDPR right-to-erasure is satisfied (person identity cannot be recovered from training labels) while model training dataset remains intact and unbiased.

target_output.title: Smart Building AI Intrusion Detection System
target_output.executive_summary: A Python 3.12 FastAPI system on AWS ECS Fargate using a transformer-based behavioral anomaly model to score access events, door sensor triggers, and camera motion alerts from smart building infrastructure in real time. SOC analysts receive WebSocket-pushed alerts with async LLM-generated incident narratives (GPT-4o via Azure OpenAI EU endpoint, anonymized inputs). Round 3 completes the architecture by adding a model fine-tuning regression gate (held-out recall benchmark before blue-green promotion) and a GDPR erasure cascade using pseudonym token rotation to preserve training data integrity while severing person linkage.
target_output.architecture_overview: Four ECS Fargate services communicate over internal VPC: event-ingestion (FastAPI, receives batched sensor events via POST from building gateway appliances), inference-service (FastAPI + PyTorch on g4dn.xlarge GPU task, scores 30-event rolling windows for anomaly probability using transformer model loaded from S3), alert-service (FastAPI, manages alert lifecycle, publishes to Redis sorted set, pushes to SOC console via WebSocket), narrative-service (FastAPI, async LLM incident narrative generation using anonymized event sequences via Azure OpenAI GPT-4o EU endpoint). TimescaleDB on RDS stores access_events and sensor_triggers hypertables. Keycloak OIDC with MFA controls all SOC console access. Weekly fine-tuning CronJob retrains transformer on labeled data with regression gate before blue-green promotion.
target_output.technology_stack: Backend: Python 3.12, FastAPI with uvicorn, PyTorch 2.3 transformer model, Pydantic v2. Database: TimescaleDB on RDS PostgreSQL 16 with KMS encryption; Redis 7 ElastiCache for alert state. Auth: Keycloak 24 with OIDC and MFA. LLM: Azure OpenAI GPT-4o via EU endpoint, HTTPS only, anonymized inputs. Infrastructure: ECS Fargate (3 CPU services + 1 GPU task g4dn.xlarge), ALB, VPC private subnets, Secrets Manager for Keycloak and Azure OpenAI credentials. CI/CD: GitLab CI with Docker, ECR, ECS blue-green deployment. Monitoring: Datadog APM + custom metrics.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Multi-Sensor Event Ingestion: access panels, door sensors, and camera motion triggers ingested via POST /events/batch from building gateway, validated and written to TimescaleDB hypertables. Real-Time Anomaly Scoring: inference-service scores rolling 30-event windows per building zone, publishing threat_incidents above threshold to alert-service. Alert Queue: SOC analysts see real-time WebSocket-pushed alerts ordered by severity score; PATCH /alerts/:id/review accepts analyst label and notes. LLM Incident Narratives: narrative-service generates human-readable summaries from anonymized event sequences, stored in incident_narratives and fetched asynchronously. Fine-Tuning Pipeline: weekly CronJob retrains transformer on analyst-labeled incidents, validates against held-out set, promotes via blue-green if recall benchmark passes. GDPR Erasure: pseudonym token rotation severs person linkage in training data without record deletion.
target_output.system_components: Building Gateway Appliances (on-premises, POST to event-ingestion API), Event Ingestion ECS Service (FastAPI, Pydantic validation, TimescaleDB write, deduplication via Redis), Inference ECS GPU Task (FastAPI + PyTorch transformer, 30-event window scoring, anomaly-events Kafka topic producer), Alert ECS Service (FastAPI, Redis sorted set alert queue, WebSocket push, Keycloak JWT auth), Narrative ECS Service (FastAPI, async Azure OpenAI calls, anonymized event extraction, incident_narratives DB writer), TimescaleDB RDS (access_events and sensor_triggers hypertables, 90-day TTL, KMS encryption), Redis 7 ElastiCache (alert queue sorted set, event deduplication set), Keycloak ECS Service (OIDC provider, RBAC realm), Fine-Tuning CronJob (weekly ECS scheduled task, regression gate, blue-green S3 model artifact promotion), Datadog APM (sidecar on all tasks), Secrets Manager (Azure OpenAI API key, Keycloak realm secret).
target_output.workflows: Real-Time Threat Detection: Building gateway POSTs batched access_events to event-ingestion service every 5 seconds. Event-ingestion deduplicates via Redis (event_id set with 5-minute TTL), validates Pydantic schema, writes to TimescaleDB access_events hypertable, and forwards event to inference-service via internal HTTP. Inference-service accumulates 30-event rolling window per zone_id, scores with PyTorch transformer producing anomaly_score 0-1.0. If score >= 0.7, creates threat_incident record and pushes alert to Redis sorted set (score as sort key). Alert-service WebSocket handler broadcasts new alerts to connected SOC console sessions filtered by analyst zone group assignment. Model Fine-Tuning with Regression Gate: GitLab scheduled pipeline triggers weekly fine-tuning ECS task. Task queries TimescaleDB for analyst-labeled threat_incidents from past 7 days (80% training split, 20% held-out). Trains transformer starting from current production model weights for 10 epochs. Evaluates on held-out set: if recall >= (current_model_recall - 0.02) AND precision >= 0.75, promotes artifact to s3://models/production/latest and triggers blue-green ECS task replacement with smoke test. If gate fails, GitLab pipeline fails and PagerDuty P2 alert fires for model-reviewer role. GDPR Erasure Cascade: DELETE /people/:id/data triggers erasure job. Job: (1) deletes access_events WHERE person_id=$1 from TimescaleDB; (2) looks up current pseudonym token from pseudonym_registry; (3) generates new pseudonym token using HMAC with new salt; (4) UPDATE model_training_labels SET pseudonym=new_token WHERE pseudonym=old_token; (5) deletes pseudonym_registry entry for person_id=$1. Person identity cannot be recovered from training labels; training records intact.
target_output.data_model: building_zones (zone_id UUID PK, building_id UUID, zone_name TEXT, floor INT, risk_level ENUM low/medium/high). access_events TimescaleDB hypertable (ts TIMESTAMPTZ, event_id UUID, zone_id FK, person_id UUID, pseudonym_token TEXT, access_type ENUM badge/keypad/forced/tailgate, outcome ENUM granted/denied/alarm, device_id UUID) -- TTL 90 days, partitioned weekly. sensor_triggers TimescaleDB hypertable (ts TIMESTAMPTZ, sensor_id UUID, zone_id FK, trigger_type ENUM door_open/motion/glass_break, severity ENUM low/medium/high) -- TTL 90 days. threat_incidents (incident_id UUID PK, zone_id FK, started_at TIMESTAMPTZ, resolved_at TIMESTAMPTZ, severity ENUM warning/critical/false_positive, anomaly_score FLOAT, model_version TEXT, analyst_id UUID). personnel_profiles (person_id UUID PK, pseudonym_token TEXT, access_level ENUM visitor/staff/contractor/executive, is_active BOOL). pseudonym_registry (person_id UUID PK, pseudonym_token TEXT, hmac_salt TEXT, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ) -- deleted on GDPR erasure. alert_dispatches (alert_id UUID PK, incident_id FK, dispatched_at TIMESTAMPTZ, ack_by UUID, ack_at TIMESTAMPTZ, channel ENUM console/sms/email). incident_narratives (narrative_id UUID PK, incident_id FK, generated_at TIMESTAMPTZ, narrative_text TEXT, llm_model_version TEXT). model_training_labels (label_id UUID PK, incident_id FK, pseudonym_token TEXT, label ENUM true_positive/false_positive, analyst_id UUID, labeled_at TIMESTAMPTZ) -- pseudonym_token rotated on GDPR erasure, never stores person_id.
target_output.api_design: POST /events/batch (building gateway, batch event ingestion, Keycloak service account auth), GET /alerts?status=pending&zone_id= (SOC analyst queue, JWT required), PATCH /alerts/:id/review {label, notes} (analyst label submission), GET /incidents/:id (incident detail with threat score and event sequence), GET /incidents/:id/narrative (async LLM narrative, returns 202 with narrative_id if generating, 200 with text when ready), DELETE /people/:id/data (GDPR erasure, system-admin role only), GET /model/metrics (model precision/recall for model-reviewer role). WebSocket: /ws/alerts?zone_group= (real-time alert push to SOC console, Keycloak JWT in header).
target_output.security_and_compliance: Keycloak OIDC realm with MFA enforced for all roles. Service-to-service communication uses Keycloak service account OIDC tokens over mTLS within ECS VPC. TimescaleDB RDS encrypted at rest with KMS CMK; TLS 1.3 in transit. Pseudonymization: person_id replaced with stable HMAC token (SHA-256 with per-deployment salt stored in Secrets Manager) before transformer model input and LLM calls. GDPR erasure cascade uses pseudonym token rotation (new HMAC salt per person erasure request) to sever identity linkage from model training labels without dataset corruption. No raw access event data sent to Azure OpenAI -- only anonymized event type, zone risk level, timestamp deltas, and anomaly score sequence. SOC 2 Type II: audit_actions TimescaleDB table logs all analyst alert reviews, label submissions, and incident accesses with Keycloak user_id and timestamp. ISO 27001 physical security: all access denial and alarm events retained minimum 3 years in incident records.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: GitLab CI pipeline: mypy strict + pytest on merge request. Docker build and push to ECR on merge to main. Staging: rolling ECS update. Production: blue-green ECS task replacement -- new task definition deployed, ALB target group switched after /health check passes and end-to-end smoke test confirms event-to-alert latency <500ms. Model fine-tuning: weekly GitLab scheduled pipeline triggers ECS CronJob; regression gate (recall >= current-0.02, precision >= 0.75 on held-out 20% label set) must pass before S3 model artifact promotion and blue-green inference-service restart. Environments: dev (single CPU task per service, stubbed inference with rule-based scorer), staging (full GPU task, mirrored TimescaleDB, 5% of production event volume replay), production (multi-AZ ECS, ALB, RDS Multi-AZ, Redis replicated). Rollback: ECS blue-green rollback restores previous task definition in under 2 minutes.
target_output.observability: Datadog APM on all ECS tasks with trace propagation across event-ingestion to inference-service to alert-service call chain. Custom Datadog metrics: inference_latency_end_to_end_p99_ms (SLA alert >500ms for 3 min -- PagerDuty P1), alert_queue_depth (P2 alert if rising for 10 min without drain), narrative_generation_latency_p95_ms, model_precision_at_threshold_weekly, false_positive_rate_7d_rolling, gdpr_erasure_completion_rate. Datadog dashboard: Threat Detection Health (alert rate by zone, false positive rate, model precision trend), Pipeline Health (inference latency percentiles, event ingestion rate, queue depth). GitLab pipeline exposes fine-tuning regression test results as CI artifact retained 90 days.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: g4dn.xlarge GPU task estimated $550/month reserved; Azure OpenAI GPT-4o at 200 incidents/day at 500 tokens ~$25/month. Total estimated $1,200/month including RDS Multi-AZ and ElastiCache. Inference-service runs single GPU task (no auto-scaling); event batching keeps inference throughput within single task capacity at projected building scale. Redis alert queue deduplication prevents alert storm amplification costs.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (10 weeks): event-ingestion and alert-service with rule-based anomaly scoring (no GPU task), Keycloak RBAC and MFA, TimescaleDB hypertables with 90-day TTL, WebSocket alert push, analyst review and label capture, GDPR erasure endpoint with pseudonym registry. Phase 2 (8 weeks): transformer model training on Phase 1 labeled data, GPU inference-service deployment with blue-green, fine-tuning CronJob with regression gate, narrative-service with Azure OpenAI pseudonymized calls. Phase 3 (6 weeks): video frame scoring integration from IP cameras, cross-building threat correlation, SIEM webhook integration for cyber-physical event fusion.
target_output.development_guidelines: Python 3.12 mypy strict mode across all FastAPI services. Pydantic v2 models for all API request/response schemas. pytest with 85% coverage gate on inference scoring logic, GDPR erasure cascade, and pseudonymization functions. GitLab CI enforces coverage gate before merge. Transformer model changes require ADR with evaluation metrics. All Keycloak realm configuration managed as code in GitLab repo. No person_id may appear in log lines -- structured logging sanitizer enforced at Datadog log pipeline.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Transformer model precision decreases on building zones with sparse event data (low-traffic floors). Mitigation: zone-specific decision threshold calibration; sparse zones use rule-based fallback scoring. Risk: Azure OpenAI EU endpoint latency variability affects narrative generation SLA. Mitigation: narrative generation is fully async with 60-second timeout; SOC analyst can view alert without narrative and refresh when ready. Tradeoff: pseudonym token rotation for GDPR erasure adds complexity to erasure workflow. Alternative (label deletion) was rejected because it creates dataset holes that bias model recall on specific access event types. Risk: g4dn.xlarge single point of failure for inference. Mitigation: blue-green deployment ensures <2 minute recovery; rule-based fallback scorer activates automatically on GPU task health check failure.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: ISS-001 resolved round 2: pseudonymization before LLM calls explicit in narrative-service. ISS-002 resolved this round: regression gate added to fine-tuning CronJob with held-out 20% label set, recall and precision thresholds, automatic rollback on gate failure. ISS-003 resolved this round: GDPR erasure cascade uses pseudonym token rotation with new HMAC salt, severing identity linkage from model_training_labels without dataset corruption. Selected Azure OpenAI EU endpoint over AWS Bedrock to satisfy GDPR data residency for EU employee behavioral data.

metadata.notes: Smart building AI intrusion detection; round 3 closes model regression gate and GDPR erasure cascade gaps; LLM narrative generation with pseudonymization.

project_goal.rationale: Primary marketing surface for an early-stage AgriTech hardware startup reaching farm equipment dealers

mvp_scope.rationale: Minimum content for dealer outreach launch

security_baseline.rationale: Static site security baseline; no server-side attack surface

frontend_stack.rationale: Astro zero-JS model suits content-heavy marketing site; MDX enables rich case study authoring

backend_stack.value: none -- static site only; Formspree for contact form submissions
backend_stack.rationale: No server-side runtime on static hosting per static_website constraint

data_platform.value: none -- all data embedded at build time from Markdown files and JSON fixtures
data_platform.rationale: No database or runtime data fetching; dealer locations compiled from JSON at build time

project.goal: Static marketing site for solar-powered LoRa agricultural sensor nodes

frontend.content: MDX case studies and blog; JSON fixtures for dealer locations and product specs

security.transport: HTTPS via Cloudflare; HSTS; CSP header blocking inline scripts

reasoner_reviews.product: Three prior rounds have progressively resolved content structure and build pipeline questions. Round 4 is a polish pass confirming the dealer map accessibility fallback and Formspree privacy policy disclosure.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Astro with Cloudflare Pages is a strong fit for this content profile. The dealer locator now uses OpenStreetMap tiles (no API key) resolved in round 3. The only remaining gap is the HTML table fallback for screen readers on the /dealers page.
reasoner_reviews.security: CSP header must allow Formspree as a connect-src directive -- this was resolved in round 3. Confirm Formspree is listed in the privacy policy as a third-party data processor. No other security concerns on a zero-server-side static site.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: Cloudflare Pages free tier supports unlimited bandwidth and 500 builds per month. Astro build for this content volume will complete in 15-25 seconds, well within the 60-second constraint.

frontend.app_structure: Astro 4 project with src/pages (product spec pages, case studies, blog, dealer-locator, contact), src/layouts (BaseLayout, CaseStudyLayout), src/components (ProductCard, DealerMap with table fallback, CaseStudyHero, ContactForm), src/content (MDX collection for case-studies and blog-posts), public/data (dealer_locations.json, product_specs.json).
frontend.pages_and_flows: / homepage with hero and use-case teaser. /products/sl-100 and /products/sl-200 generated from product_specs.json. /case-studies/* from MDX content collection. /dealers with Leaflet map and HTML table fallback. /contact with Formspree form. /blog/* MDX posts.
frontend.performance_notes: Astro renders all pages as zero-JS HTML by default. Leaflet.js loaded only on /dealers via client:load. Tailwind purge removes unused CSS. Cloudflare CDN caches all assets globally.
frontend.accessibility_notes: Dealer map includes HTML table fallback inside noscript tag and as sr-only region listing company_name, city, phone, website for screen readers. All images have descriptive alt text.

security.auth_design: No authentication required. Contact form CSRF protection handled by Formspree origin check.
security.abuse_prevention: Formspree handles contact form rate limiting and spam filtering. No server-side surface to protect.
security.security_risks: Primary risk is CSP misconfiguration. Mitigated by explicit connect-src and img-src directives in Cloudflare Pages _headers file including https://formspree.io and OpenStreetMap tile CDN.

data.entities: product_pages (product_id, name, model, power_source, lora_range_km, sensing_capabilities, battery_life_days, price_usd, datasheet_url) stored in product_specs.json. case_studies (slug, title, crop_type, region, sensor_model, challenge, solution, results_summary, hero_image) as MDX. dealer_locations (dealer_id, company_name, city, state_province, country, lat, lng, phone, website) in dealer_locations.json. blog_posts as MDX files.
data.storage_design: All data in GitHub repository as JSON and MDX files. No database. dealer_locations.json embedded in DealerMap component at build. product_specs.json used by getStaticPaths to generate /products/* pages.
data.migration_notes: No database migration. Dealer updates require JSON file edit and commit triggering Cloudflare Pages rebuild.

devops.deployment_topology: Single Cloudflare Pages project connected to GitHub. Every push to main triggers production build and deploy. PRs trigger preview deploys at unique Cloudflare Pages URLs.
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: bun install, bun run build (astro build), astro check (TypeScript), broken-link-checker on dist/. Cloudflare Pages auto-deploys on GitHub push. Branch protection requires Actions passing before merge to main.
devops.cost_controls: Cloudflare Pages free tier: unlimited bandwidth, 500 builds/month. Formspree free tier: 50 submissions/month. Total: $0 at launch.

target_output.title: Solar LoRa Agricultural Sensor Marketing Site
target_output.executive_summary: An Astro 4 static marketing website for a solar-powered LoRa agricultural sensor startup deployed on Cloudflare Pages with zero server-side runtime. Product spec pages for SL-100 and SL-200 sensor nodes are generated from product_specs.json at build time. MDX case studies cover soil moisture monitoring, frost detection, and irrigation automation. The dealer locator uses Leaflet.js with OpenStreetMap tiles and a full HTML table fallback for screen readers. Contact inquiries post to Formspree. Cloudflare Analytics provides cookieless traffic insights without a consent banner.
target_output.architecture_overview: Astro 4 build process reads MDX content collections and JSON fixtures to generate a fully static HTML bundle. No JavaScript ships to the browser for core page content -- Leaflet.js loads only on /dealers via Astro client:load directive. Cloudflare Pages deploys the dist/ folder to its global edge network. The dealer locator page embeds dealer_locations.json at build time, rendering both an interactive Leaflet map and a complete HTML table for accessibility. Contact form submissions go directly from the browser to Formspree via HTTPS POST; no server-side form handler exists. GitHub Actions validates builds with astro check and broken-link-checker before Cloudflare Pages deploys.
target_output.technology_stack: Static generator: Astro 4 with TypeScript. Styling: Tailwind CSS 3 (purged at build). Content: MDX for case studies and blog; JSON fixtures for product specs and dealer data. Map: Leaflet.js 1.9 with OpenStreetMap tiles (no API key). Form: Formspree (third-party, listed in privacy policy). Analytics: Cloudflare Web Analytics (cookieless). Hosting: Cloudflare Pages. CI: GitHub Actions with bun, astro check, broken-link-checker.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Product Spec Pages: /products/sl-100 and /products/sl-200 generated via getStaticPaths from product_specs.json showing LoRa range (15km line-of-sight), solar charging specs, sensing capabilities (soil moisture, temperature, GPS), and datasheet PDF download. Case Studies: /case-studies/* from MDX collection covering almond orchard frost detection (California), dryland wheat soil moisture (Kansas), and vineyard irrigation automation (Chile). Dealer Locator: /dealers page with Leaflet.js interactive map AND HTML table listing company_name, city, state_province, country, phone, website for screen reader and noscript access. Contact Form: Formspree-hosted form for dealer inquiries. Blog: MDX posts for product announcements and agronomy content.
target_output.system_components: GitHub Repository (MDX files, JSON fixtures, Astro components as source of truth), GitHub Actions Workflow (bun install, astro build, astro check TypeScript, broken-link-checker on dist/), Cloudflare Pages (auto-deploy on push to main, PR preview URLs, global CDN), Astro 4 Build Engine (static HTML from content collections and JSON), DealerMap Astro Component (Leaflet.js map + noscript HTML table fallback from dealer_locations.json), Formspree (third-party form handler, HTTPS POST from browser, listed in privacy policy), Cloudflare Web Analytics (cookieless, no consent banner).
target_output.workflows: Content Publishing: Editor commits MDX or JSON update to GitHub. GitHub Actions runs bun install, astro build, astro check, broken-link-checker. Branch protection requires passing checks before merge to main. Cloudflare Pages detects GitHub push and deploys updated static bundle within 2 minutes of merge. Dealer Locator Interaction: Visitor loads /dealers; Astro-generated HTML includes the full dealer HTML table (screen-reader accessible, noscript-safe) and the Leaflet map div. After page paint, Leaflet.js loads and renders interactive markers over OpenStreetMap tiles. All dealer data came from dealer_locations.json at build -- zero runtime API calls.
target_output.data_model: product_pages stored in src/data/product_specs.json: (product_id STRING, name STRING, model STRING, power_source STRING, lora_range_km INT, sensing_capabilities STRING array, battery_life_days INT, price_usd INT, datasheet_url STRING). case_studies as MDX in src/content/case-studies/: (slug, title, crop_type, region, sensor_model, challenge, solution, results_summary, hero_image). dealer_locations in src/data/dealer_locations.json: (dealer_id STRING, company_name STRING, city STRING, state_province STRING, country STRING, lat FLOAT, lng FLOAT, phone STRING, website STRING). blog_posts as MDX in src/content/blog/. No runtime database or server-side storage.
target_output.api_design: No server-side API. Build-time data: getStaticPaths reads product_specs.json to generate /products/* routes. Astro.glob on case-studies MDX generates /case-studies/* routes. dealer_locations.json imported directly into DealerMap.astro. External: Formspree form action https://formspree.io/{form-id} receives browser POST on contact form submit. No API keys in client bundle (OpenStreetMap free; Formspree form ID is intentionally public).
target_output.security_and_compliance: HTTPS enforced by Cloudflare Pages automatic SSL. Cloudflare Pages _headers file: Strict-Transport-Security max-age=31536000; Content-Security-Policy default-src 'self'; script-src 'self'; style-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline'; connect-src 'self' https://formspree.io; img-src 'self' https://*.tile.openstreetmap.org data:. No cookies set by the site. Cloudflare Web Analytics is cookieless and GDPR-exempt. Privacy policy MDX page lists Formspree (https://formspree.io) as sole third-party data processor for contact inquiry submissions with link to Formspree privacy policy.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: GitHub push to main: GitHub Actions runs bun install --frozen-lockfile, bun run build (astro build), astro check, broken-link-checker on dist/. Branch protection requires passing checks before merge. Cloudflare Pages webhook triggers on GitHub push and deploys static bundle globally. PR preview: Cloudflare Pages deploys every PR branch to a unique preview URL for content review. Production: solar-sensors.com via Cloudflare custom domain with automatic certificate renewal. Build time: 15-20 seconds. Rollback: revert Git commit triggers new Cloudflare Pages build automatically.
target_output.observability: Cloudflare Web Analytics dashboard: page views by URL, top referrers, visitor countries, device breakdown. Key content signals: /dealers page views (dealer interest), /products/sl-100 and /products/sl-200 spec views, /contact views vs Formspree submission rate. No analytics scripts beyond Cloudflare first-party -- no consent banner required.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Total hosting cost $0 on Cloudflare Pages free tier (unlimited bandwidth, 500 builds/month, global CDN). Formspree free tier 50 submissions/month; upgrade to $10/month when lead volume exceeds threshold. Domain approximately $12/year. No infrastructure scaling needed -- Cloudflare CDN handles any traffic spike without configuration.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 Launch (3 weeks): homepage, SL-100 and SL-200 product spec pages, three MDX case studies, dealer locator with Leaflet map and HTML table fallback, Formspree contact form, GitHub Actions CI, Cloudflare Pages deployment. Phase 2 (4 weeks post-launch): blog section with agronomy content, FAQ page, datasheet PDF hosting, product comparison table. Phase 3 (2 months): localized Spanish and Portuguese versions via Astro i18n routing; distributor portal is a separate authenticated application outside this static site scope.
target_output.development_guidelines: Astro TypeScript strict mode. Tailwind classes only -- no inline styles. Content editors work in src/content/ MDX and src/data/ JSON only. New case studies require a 1200x630 WebP hero image in public/images/case-studies/. dealer_locations.json lat/lng validated with geocoding tool before commit. All images must have descriptive alt text. Accessibility: DealerMap component must maintain the HTML table fallback -- removal is a breaking change requiring ADR. GitHub branch protection prevents direct push to main.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Formspree free tier 50 submissions/month reached if launch generates strong dealer interest. Mitigation: monitor Formspree dashboard weekly; upgrade to paid plan proactively at $10/month. Risk: OpenStreetMap tile server rate-limit on unusually high /dealers traffic. Mitigation: Leaflet caches tiles in browser localStorage; OSM tile policy acceptable for this traffic profile. Tradeoff: static JSON for dealer locations means data freshness requires a commit and rebuild rather than an admin panel. Accepted because startup team is small and build time is 20 seconds. No technical lock-in: Astro project portable to any static host.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: ISS-001 resolved round 3: CSP connect-src https://formspree.io in _headers. ISS-002 resolved round 3: OpenStreetMap tiles eliminate API key exposure. ISS-003 resolved this round: HTML table fallback added to DealerMap for screen readers; Formspree added to privacy policy. Selected Cloudflare Analytics over Google Analytics to avoid consent banner. Confirmed Formspree over serverless form handler to eliminate backend infrastructure.

metadata.notes: AgriTech static marketing site; round 4 adds dealer map accessibility table fallback; architecture fully resolved by round 3.

project_goal.value: Replace a legacy FTP-based batch import of water meter readings from a municipal smart meter network with a real-time AMQP-driven ingestion layer enabling sub-hourly leak detection and billing accuracy improvements
project_goal.rationale: Core modernization driver is reducing unbilled water loss from undetected leaks and improving billing cycle accuracy from monthly to daily

target_users.value: Municipal utility operations team, field technicians receiving leak alerts, and billing system consuming daily consumption data
target_users.rationale: Primary consumers of pipeline output: operations dashboard, field technician mobile app, and billing ERP integration

project_class.rationale: Core deliverable is an ingestion, validation, and analytics pipeline replacing a legacy batch process

capabilities.rationale: AMQP ingestion, TimescaleDB storage, leak detection batch queries, and billing system integration

complexity_level.rationale: Legacy FTP co-existence during migration, AMQP protocol integration with smart meter vendor, and real-time continuous query leak detection

risk_level.rationale: Residential consumption data is personal under GDPR; pipeline failure delays billing but has no safety consequence

data_sensitivity.rationale: Hourly residential water consumption data reveals occupancy patterns and constitutes personal data under GDPR

external_exposure.rationale: Pipeline runs within municipal GCP project; no public internet exposure

access_model.value: Custom JWT issued by internal identity service; service accounts for pipeline components; field technician access via existing mobile app auth
access_model.rationale: Municipal IT does not use a cloud identity provider; custom JWT service is the existing standard

feature_scope.value: AMQP message ingestion from smart meter concentrators, read deduplication, TimescaleDB storage in district-partitioned hypertables, 15-minute consumption delta continuous query for leak detection, daily billing export to ERP via CSV, legacy FTP parallel run during migration, field technician leak alert API
feature_scope.rationale: Full feature set for production modernized pipeline

mvp_scope.value: AMQP ingestion with deduplication, TimescaleDB hypertable writes, 15-minute delta leak detection query, leak-suspicion flag API for field technicians
mvp_scope.rationale: Leak detection is the highest-value improvement over the legacy FTP system

security_baseline.value: TLS 1.2 for AMQP connections from meter concentrators; custom JWT for API access; no residential meter_id or consumption data exposed outside GCP project VPC
security_baseline.rationale: Municipal IT security baseline; data residency within GCP project required

frontend_stack.value: none -- pipeline only; leak alerts consumed by existing field technician mobile app via REST API
frontend_stack.rationale: No custom frontend; existing mobile app integrates via new leak alert API

backend_stack.value: Go 1.22 with custom AMQP consumer and stream processor; no external framework
backend_stack.rationale: Go selected for low-memory AMQP consumer performance at high message rates; team has Go expertise

data_platform.value: TimescaleDB on Cloud SQL for PostgreSQL for meter read time-series with hypertable partitioning by district
data_platform.rationale: TimescaleDB continuous aggregates and continuous queries are purpose-built for the 15-minute delta leak detection pattern

hosting_target.value: GKE Autopilot within municipal GCP project; no public load balancer
hosting_target.rationale: Autopilot simplifies node management; GCP aligns with existing municipal cloud contract

privacy_retention_policy.value: Meter reads retained 7 years for billing dispute resolution per municipal regulation; 15-minute aggregates retained indefinitely; raw reads anonymized after 7 years
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: GDPR and municipal water billing retention requirements

future_scope.value: Predictive pipe failure scoring using historical leak patterns, integration with GIS mapping for district-level loss visualization, tariff tier automation based on daily consumption
future_scope.rationale: Phase 2 analytics capabilities after AMQP pipeline is stable

constraints.value: Legacy FTP batch must run in parallel until 100% of meter concentrators are upgraded to AMQP; pipeline must handle 18-hour offline backlog when concentrators reconnect; GCP project budget under $600/month
constraints.rationale: Concentrator firmware upgrades are staggered over 6 months; backlog handling is a hard operational requirement

observability_baseline.value: Grafana dashboards with Prometheus metrics from all GKE services; Cloud Monitoring for GKE and Cloud SQL health
observability_baseline.rationale: Existing GCP-based Grafana/Prometheus stack; no new tooling budget

execution_preference.value: Cloud Build CI/CD with GitOps Helm chart deployment to GKE Autopilot
execution_preference.rationale: GCP Cloud Build is the municipal IT standard for GKE deployments

compliance_context.value: GDPR for residential customer consumption data: consent for data collection via utility service agreement, right-to-erasure within 30 days for service cancellations, municipal data retention regulations requiring 7-year billing records
compliance_context.rationale: data_sensitivity=personal triggers GDPR compliance obligations for residential meter read data

project.goal: Replace legacy FTP batch meter imports with real-time AMQP pipeline for sub-hourly leak detection and billing accuracy
project.target_users: Utility operations, field technicians, billing ERP
project.timeline: Production by end of Q3 with FTP parallel run until Q4

backend.framework: Custom AMQP consumer and HTTP API service
backend.api: Internal REST for leak alert query and billing export trigger

security.auth: Custom JWT for API endpoints; TLS 1.2 for AMQP broker connections
security.encryption: GCP CMEK for Cloud SQL; VPC-only network access

data.db: TimescaleDB on Cloud SQL for PostgreSQL
data.cache: none required; deduplication via TimescaleDB UNIQUE constraint

devops.hosting: GKE Autopilot in GCP municipal project

constraints.ftp_parallel_run: legacy FTP runs until all concentrators upgraded

reasoner_reviews.product: The modernization goal is clear: replace monthly billing batch cycles with sub-hourly consumption data and real-time leak detection. The 15-minute delta continuous query is the right technical approach for the leak-suspicion flag. The legacy FTP parallel run constraint is realistic given staggered concentrator firmware upgrades.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Round 1 plan established the AMQP consumer and TimescaleDB hypertable structure but did not address the 18-hour backlog reconnect handling -- when a concentrator comes back online after an 18-hour outage, it replays accumulated reads at burst rate. The AMQP consumer must handle message rate spikes gracefully with backpressure and idempotent deduplication. The TimescaleDB UNIQUE constraint on (meter_id, read_at) provides deduplication but INSERT ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING must be explicit.
reasoner_reviews.security: GDPR right-to-erasure must cascade from meter_reads to consumption_intervals when a residential service account is cancelled. The erasure must complete within 30 days. TimescaleDB chunk-based deletion for a single service_account_id is non-trivial -- a background job scanning chunk ranges by service_account_id FK is required.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: GCP budget of $600/month is achievable: Cloud SQL for PostgreSQL with TimescaleDB extension on db-custom-2-7680 instance ($140/month), GKE Autopilot workloads (~$200/month at projected message volume), and Grafana/Prometheus on a small GKE workload. FTP parallel run adds minimal cost as the legacy process writes to a separate staging table.

backend.service_design: Three GKE Autopilot deployments: (1) amqp-consumer: Go service subscribing to meter.readings.# AMQP topic, validates message schema, deduplicates via TimescaleDB INSERT ON CONFLICT, writes to meter_reads hypertable. (2) leak-detector: Go service running continuous query against consumption_intervals_15min materialized view, evaluating per-property baseline deviation, writing leak_flags records. (3) billing-exporter: Go CronJob running nightly, querying daily consumption totals from TimescaleDB continuous aggregate, writing CSV to GCS bucket for ERP pickup. Legacy FTP bridge: separate Go CronJob polling FTP server, parsing CSV, inserting to same meter_reads table with source='ftp' tag during migration period.
backend.api_patterns: REST API (chi router): GET /meters/:id/leak-flags?since= (field technician leak query), GET /districts/:id/consumption?date= (daily district totals for operations dashboard), POST /export/billing (manual billing export trigger), GET /health (Kubernetes liveness). All endpoints require custom JWT in Authorization header.
backend.failure_handling: AMQP consumer uses manual ACK after successful TimescaleDB write. On consumer crash, unacked messages return to AMQP queue and are reprocessed (idempotent via ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING). Dead-letter queue meter.readings.dlq for messages failing schema validation after 3 retries. Leak detector falls back to previous 24-hour flag state on TimescaleDB query timeout.
backend.scaling_notes: GKE Autopilot horizontal pod autoscaling on amqp-consumer based on AMQP queue depth metric (target depth 5000 messages). At 18-hour backlog reconnect, concentrators replay ~3000 reads each; consumer scales from 2 to 6 pods within 3 minutes.

security.auth_design: Custom JWT signed with RS256; HS256 rejected. JWT payload includes service_id and role claims. API service validates JWT signature against public key stored in GCP Secret Manager. AMQP broker (RabbitMQ on GKE) uses TLS 1.2 with per-concentrator client credentials from GCP Secret Manager.
security.authorization_model: Role-based: field-technician (GET /meters/:id/leak-flags only, filtered to assigned district), operations-analyst (read-only all districts), billing-system (GET /districts/:id/consumption and POST /export/billing), system-admin (all endpoints). JWT role claim enforced in chi middleware.
security.privacy_controls: GDPR erasure job: triggered on service account cancellation event. Job deletes meter_reads WHERE service_account_id=$1 from TimescaleDB (scanning all relevant chunks), cascades to consumption_intervals, leak_flags, and billing_periods. Erasure logged to gdpr_erasure_log table. Completed within 30-day SLA tracked by Grafana metric.
security.audit_and_logging_controls: All API requests logged to GCP Cloud Logging with service_account_id masked to last-4 digits. Leak flag creation and billing export triggers written to audit_actions table. GDPR erasure completions logged with timestamp and record counts.

data.entities: meters (meter_id UUID PK, service_account_id FK, district_zone_id FK, address_encrypted TEXT, meter_type ENUM amqp/ftp_legacy, last_read_at TIMESTAMPTZ, is_active BOOL). meter_reads TimescaleDB hypertable (read_at TIMESTAMPTZ, meter_id UUID, district_zone_id UUID, volume_liters FLOAT, source ENUM amqp/ftp_legacy, ingested_at TIMESTAMPTZ, UNIQUE (meter_id, read_at)). service_accounts (account_id UUID PK, customer_name_encrypted TEXT, address_id FK, tariff_tier ENUM residential/commercial, is_active BOOL). consumption_intervals TimescaleDB continuous aggregate (interval_start TIMESTAMPTZ, meter_id UUID, district_zone_id UUID, total_liters FLOAT, interval_minutes INT default 15). leak_flags (flag_id UUID PK, meter_id FK, service_account_id FK, detected_at TIMESTAMPTZ, delta_pct_above_baseline FLOAT, severity ENUM low/medium/high, resolved_at TIMESTAMPTZ, acknowledged_by TEXT). billing_periods (period_id UUID PK, service_account_id FK, period_start DATE, period_end DATE, total_liters FLOAT, tariff_tier, exported_at TIMESTAMPTZ). district_zones (zone_id UUID PK, zone_name TEXT, district TEXT, pipe_age_years INT, concentrator_count INT).
data.storage_design: TimescaleDB hypertable meter_reads partitioned by week with chunk compression after 30 days. Continuous aggregate consumption_intervals_15min refreshes every 15 minutes. Retention policy retains raw meter_reads 7 years; consumption_intervals retained indefinitely per municipal regulation.
data.consistency_notes: INSERT ON CONFLICT (meter_id, read_at) DO NOTHING on meter_reads ensures idempotent ingestion from both AMQP and legacy FTP source during parallel run. FTP reads tagged with source='ftp_legacy'; AMQP reads with source='amqp'. Deduplication occurs naturally when same read arrives from both sources.

devops.deployment_topology: GKE Autopilot cluster in GCP municipal project (us-central1). Three Autopilot workloads: amqp-consumer (2-6 replicas HPA), leak-detector (1 replica), billing-exporter (CronJob). RabbitMQ AMQP broker as StatefulSet. Cloud SQL for PostgreSQL with TimescaleDB extension (db-custom-2-7680, us-central1-a, automatic backups).
devops.ci_cd_design: Cloud Build trigger on push to main: go build, go test ./..., Docker build and push to Artifact Registry, Helm values.yaml update, Cloud Build Deploy step applies Helm chart to GKE. Staging environment mirrors production topology at 20% scale for integration testing. FTP bridge CronJob deployed alongside during migration phase only.
devops.observability_stack: Prometheus scrapes all GKE pod /metrics endpoints. Grafana dashboards: AMQP ingestion rate (reads/sec by district), consumer queue depth (HPA trigger), leak flag rate (flags/hour by district), billing export completion status, GDPR erasure queue lag. Cloud Monitoring for Cloud SQL CPU, storage, and connection count. Alertmanager: queue depth >10K for 5 min triggers PagerDuty P1.

ISS-001.title: 18-hour backlog reconnect handling not designed
ISS-001.detail: Round 1 plan has no HPA strategy or backpressure for the 18-hour concentrator offline backlog. When a concentrator reconnects, it replays accumulated reads at burst rate that can overwhelm the amqp-consumer.

history.note: Identified during architect review of round 1 plan

ISS-002.title: GDPR erasure cascade to TimescaleDB not designed
ISS-002.detail: Right-to-erasure for cancelled service accounts must cascade from meter_reads hypertable to consumption_intervals and billing_periods. TimescaleDB chunk scanning by service_account_id FK is non-trivial.

ISS-003.title: FTP parallel run deduplication not specified
ISS-003.detail: During FTP and AMQP parallel run, the same meter read may arrive from both sources. Round 1 plan does not specify how duplicate detection prevents double-counting in billing totals.

focus_issues.title: 18-hour backlog reconnect handling not designed
focus_issues.detail: No HPA or backpressure for 18-hour concentrator reconnect burst in round 1.

focus_issues.title: FTP parallel run deduplication not specified
focus_issues.detail: Same meter read arriving from FTP and AMQP may double-count in billing.

previous_plan.title: Municipal Smart Meter AMQP Pipeline v1
previous_plan.executive_summary: Go AMQP consumer on GKE Autopilot ingesting smart meter reads from RabbitMQ into TimescaleDB hypertable with 15-minute continuous aggregate for leak detection. Legacy FTP bridge CronJob runs in parallel during migration.
previous_plan.architecture_overview: amqp-consumer GKE Deployment subscribes to RabbitMQ meter.readings topic, writes to meter_reads TimescaleDB hypertable. Continuous aggregate computes 15-minute consumption intervals. Leak detector compares intervals against per-property baseline. No HPA or backlog burst handling specified.
previous_plan.data_model: meter_reads TimescaleDB hypertable (read_at, meter_id, volume_liters). No UNIQUE constraint on (meter_id, read_at). FTP bridge inserts from CSV without deduplication against AMQP reads.
previous_plan.security_and_compliance: Custom JWT for API. TLS on AMQP. No GDPR erasure cascade design.

target_output.thinking_summary: Round 2 addresses the two focus issues: ISS-001 requires AMQP consumer HPA on RabbitMQ queue depth metric to handle the 18-hour backlog burst (concentrators replaying ~3000 reads each on reconnect), with manual ACK ensuring no message loss during scale-out; ISS-003 requires a UNIQUE constraint on (meter_id, read_at) with INSERT ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING, making both AMQP and FTP legacy ingestion idempotent and preventing double-counting in billing totals. ISS-002 GDPR erasure cascade is addressed with a background CronJob design. The core hypertable and continuous aggregate design from round 1 is retained.

fix_report.action_taken: Added RabbitMQ queue depth custom metric (rabbitmq_queue_messages) to GKE HPA for amqp-consumer with targetAverageValue 5000 messages. Consumer scales from 2 to 6 replicas within 3 minutes of burst onset. Manual AMQP ACK pattern preserves message ordering and prevents data loss during scale-out. Documented in deployment_and_operations and workflows sections.
fix_report.expected_outcome: 18-hour concentrator backlog (approximately 3000 reads per concentrator) processed within 45 minutes after reconnect with 6-replica scale-out. No message loss due to manual ACK with requeue on consumer crash.

fix_report.action_taken: Added UNIQUE (meter_id, read_at) constraint to meter_reads TimescaleDB hypertable. Both AMQP consumer and FTP bridge now use INSERT INTO meter_reads ... ON CONFLICT (meter_id, read_at) DO NOTHING. Source column (amqp or ftp_legacy) retains the first-ingested source tag. This makes dual-source ingestion idempotent and prevents billing double-counting during the parallel FTP migration period.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Duplicate reads from FTP and AMQP parallel run silently discarded. Billing totals computed from consumption_intervals continuous aggregate reflect correct per-meter consumption without double-counting.

target_output.title: Municipal Smart Meter AMQP Ingestion Pipeline
target_output.executive_summary: A Go 1.22 pipeline on GKE Autopilot replacing legacy FTP batch meter imports with real-time AMQP ingestion from municipal smart meter concentrators. TimescaleDB on Cloud SQL stores meter_reads in district-partitioned hypertables with a 15-minute continuous aggregate powering sub-hourly leak detection. An 18-hour concentrator backlog is handled via RabbitMQ queue depth HPA scaling amqp-consumer from 2 to 6 replicas. FTP and AMQP parallel ingestion is made idempotent by UNIQUE (meter_id, read_at) with ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING. GDPR erasure for cancelled residential service accounts runs as a background GKE CronJob with a 30-day SLA.
target_output.architecture_overview: RabbitMQ AMQP broker (GKE StatefulSet) receives meter readings from smart meter concentrators over TLS 1.2, routing to meter.readings.# topic. The amqp-consumer GKE Deployment subscribes with manual ACK, validates message schema, and writes to the meter_reads TimescaleDB hypertable using INSERT ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING for idempotent dual-source ingestion. GKE HPA scales amqp-consumer on RabbitMQ queue depth metric to absorb 18-hour backlog reconnect bursts. The leak-detector GKE Deployment runs a continuous query against the consumption_intervals_15min TimescaleDB materialized view, comparing 15-minute deltas against per-property baselines and writing leak_flags records. A billing-exporter CronJob queries daily consumption totals and writes CSV to GCS for ERP pickup. The legacy FTP bridge CronJob runs in parallel during the concentrator migration period, inserting reads to the same meter_reads table tagged source='ftp_legacy'. A GDPR erasure CronJob processes service cancellation events, deleting meter reads and cascading to related tables within 30 days.
target_output.technology_stack: Go 1.22 for all pipeline services (amqp-consumer, leak-detector, billing-exporter, ftp-bridge, gdpr-eraser). RabbitMQ 3.13 AMQP broker as GKE StatefulSet. TimescaleDB extension on Cloud SQL for PostgreSQL 15 (db-custom-2-7680). GCP Cloud Storage for billing CSV exports. GKE Autopilot (us-central1). Cloud Build CI/CD. Artifact Registry for Docker images. Grafana + Prometheus for observability. GCP Secret Manager for JWT signing keys and AMQP credentials.
target_output.functional_feature_map: AMQP Ingestion: meter readings from concentrators ingested via RabbitMQ at up to 2000 msg/sec peak, validated, deduplicated via ON CONFLICT, written to meter_reads hypertable with sub-second latency. Backlog Burst Handling: HPA on queue depth scales amqp-consumer to 6 replicas during 18-hour reconnect burst, processing accumulated backlog within 45 minutes. Leak Detection: 15-minute consumption delta continuous query compares per-property intervals against rolling 30-day baseline; readings exceeding 2 standard deviations trigger leak_flags with severity classification. Field Technician API: GET /meters/:id/leak-flags returns active leak suspicions for a meter, filtered by technician's assigned district zone. Billing Export: nightly CronJob queries daily_consumption_totals continuous aggregate and writes CSV to GCS bucket for ERP billing system. FTP Parallel Run: legacy FTP bridge CronJob parses CSV from FTP server and inserts to same meter_reads table with ON CONFLICT deduplication until all concentrators are upgraded. GDPR Erasure: background CronJob processes cancellation events, deleting meter_reads and cascading to derived tables within 30 days.
target_output.system_components: RabbitMQ AMQP Broker (GKE StatefulSet, TLS 1.2, meter.readings.# topic, meter.readings.dlq dead-letter), amqp-consumer GKE Deployment (Go, manual ACK, ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING writes, HPA on queue depth 5000 target, 2-6 replicas), leak-detector GKE Deployment (Go, continuous query on consumption_intervals_15min, leak_flags writer), billing-exporter GKE CronJob (nightly, daily_consumption_totals query, GCS CSV write), ftp-bridge GKE CronJob (Go, FTP CSV parser, same ON CONFLICT ingestion -- migration phase only), gdpr-eraser GKE CronJob (Go, 30-day SLA erasure of meter_reads and related tables), TimescaleDB on Cloud SQL (meter_reads hypertable, continuous aggregates, 7-year retention), GCS Bucket (billing CSV exports for ERP), Prometheus + Grafana (observability), GCP Secret Manager (JWT keys, AMQP credentials, FTP credentials).
target_output.workflows: Real-Time Meter Read Ingestion: Smart meter concentrator publishes reading to RabbitMQ meter.readings.{district_id}.{meter_id} with TLS. amqp-consumer validates JSON schema (meter_id UUID, read_at RFC3339, volume_liters FLOAT). On validation failure after 3 retries, message routed to meter.readings.dlq. On success, executes INSERT INTO meter_reads (read_at, meter_id, district_zone_id, volume_liters, source) VALUES ($1,$2,$3,$4,'amqp') ON CONFLICT (meter_id, read_at) DO NOTHING. Manual AMQP ACK sent after successful INSERT. Concentrator Backlog Reconnect: Concentrator offline 18 hours (e.g., firmware upgrade) then reconnects. RabbitMQ queue depth rises rapidly as concentrator replays accumulated readings. Prometheus rabbitmq_queue_messages metric exceeds HPA target of 5000. GKE Autopilot scales amqp-consumer from 2 to 6 replicas within 3 minutes. Each replica processes its partition of the queue; all writes are idempotent via ON CONFLICT. Queue drains within 45 minutes. HPA scales back to 2 replicas after queue depth stabilizes below 1000. 15-Minute Leak Detection Query: leak-detector runs every 15 minutes via time.Ticker. Queries: SELECT meter_id, SUM(volume_liters) as interval_liters FROM meter_reads WHERE read_at > NOW() - INTERVAL '15 minutes' GROUP BY meter_id. Compares against 30-day rolling baseline from consumption_intervals_15min aggregate. Meters where interval_liters exceeds (baseline_mean + 2 * baseline_stddev) receive a leak_flag record with severity based on deviation magnitude.
target_output.data_model: meters (meter_id UUID PK, service_account_id FK, district_zone_id FK, address_encrypted TEXT, meter_type ENUM amqp/ftp_legacy, last_read_at TIMESTAMPTZ, is_active BOOL). meter_reads TimescaleDB hypertable (read_at TIMESTAMPTZ, meter_id UUID, district_zone_id UUID, volume_liters FLOAT, source ENUM amqp/ftp_legacy, ingested_at TIMESTAMPTZ, UNIQUE (meter_id, read_at)) -- partitioned by week, chunk compression after 30 days, TTL 7 years. service_accounts (account_id UUID PK, customer_name_encrypted TEXT, tariff_tier ENUM residential/commercial, is_active BOOL). consumption_intervals (interval_start TIMESTAMPTZ, meter_id UUID, district_zone_id UUID, total_liters FLOAT, interval_minutes INT) -- TimescaleDB continuous aggregate refreshing every 15 minutes. daily_consumption_totals -- TimescaleDB continuous aggregate on consumption_intervals aggregated to calendar day, used for billing export. leak_flags (flag_id UUID PK, meter_id FK, service_account_id FK, detected_at TIMESTAMPTZ, delta_pct_above_baseline FLOAT, severity ENUM low/medium/high, resolved_at TIMESTAMPTZ, acknowledged_by TEXT). billing_periods (period_id UUID PK, service_account_id FK, period_start DATE, period_end DATE, total_liters FLOAT, tariff_tier, exported_at TIMESTAMPTZ). district_zones (zone_id UUID PK, zone_name TEXT, district TEXT, pipe_age_years INT, concentrator_count INT). gdpr_erasure_log (log_id UUID PK, service_account_id UUID, requested_at TIMESTAMPTZ, completed_at TIMESTAMPTZ, records_deleted_count INT, status ENUM pending/completed/failed).
target_output.api_design: REST API (Go chi router): GET /meters/:id/leak-flags?since=&resolved=false (field technician leak query, JWT role field-technician, filtered to assigned district_zone_id), GET /districts/:id/consumption?date= (daily district total from continuous aggregate, role operations-analyst), GET /meters/:id/reads?from=&to= (raw read history for billing disputes, role billing-system), POST /export/billing?date= (manual billing export trigger, role billing-system), GET /health (liveness check, no auth). All endpoints require custom JWT RS256 Bearer token. JWT validation checks signature against public key from Secret Manager and enforces role claim for each endpoint.
target_output.security_and_compliance: Custom JWT RS256 signed by internal JWT service; public key stored in GCP Secret Manager and cached with 1-hour refresh in API service. RabbitMQ broker TLS 1.2 with per-concentrator credential pairs from Secret Manager. Cloud SQL encrypted with GCP CMEK; all data within GCP VPC -- no public IP on Cloud SQL instance. GDPR erasure CronJob: triggered by service cancellation event (GCP Pub/Sub message). Job executes: DELETE FROM meter_reads WHERE meter_id IN (SELECT meter_id FROM meters WHERE service_account_id=$1) for each TimescaleDB chunk in range; DELETE FROM leak_flags WHERE service_account_id=$1; DELETE FROM billing_periods WHERE service_account_id=$1; UPDATE service_accounts SET customer_name_encrypted=NULL, is_active=false WHERE account_id=$1. Erasure logged to gdpr_erasure_log with completion timestamp and record counts. Grafana metric gdpr_erasure_completion_days alerts if any erasure job exceeds 25 days (5-day buffer before 30-day SLA breach). API access logs shipped to GCP Cloud Logging with service_account_id masked to last-4 characters.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: Cloud Build trigger on push to main: go build ./..., go test ./... (unit tests for AMQP consumer, leak detection query logic, billing export), Docker build and push to GCP Artifact Registry, Cloud Build Deploy step applies Helm chart to GKE Autopilot target namespace. Staging environment: GKE Autopilot at 20% scale, RabbitMQ single-node, Cloud SQL dev instance, integration tests replay 24 hours of historical meter reads to validate deduplication and leak detection logic. Production: GKE Autopilot us-central1, Cloud SQL db-custom-2-7680 with automatic backups daily and PITR, RabbitMQ StatefulSet with persistent volume. FTP bridge CronJob deployed with feature flag env var enabling it only during migration phase (ENABLE_FTP_BRIDGE=true). Rollback: Cloud Build deployment revision rollback restores previous Helm release; no migration rollback needed as meter_reads UNIQUE constraint is additive.
target_output.observability: Prometheus scrapes all GKE pod /metrics endpoints every 30 seconds. Key Grafana metrics: amqp_consumer_messages_per_sec (by district, broken out by source=amqp vs ftp_legacy during parallel run), rabbitmq_queue_messages (HPA trigger, PagerDuty P1 alert >20K sustained 5 min), leak_flags_created_per_hour (P2 alert on sudden spike indicating meter malfunction), billing_export_last_success_timestamp (P2 alert if >25 hours), gdpr_erasure_completion_days (P2 alert if any job >25 days). Cloud Monitoring dashboards for Cloud SQL CPU and connection count. Grafana alert: FTP bridge CronJob failure triggers P1 during migration period (billing continuity risk).
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Cloud SQL db-custom-2-7680 in us-central1: ~$140/month. GKE Autopilot workloads (amqp-consumer 2-6 replicas, leak-detector, billing-exporter, gdpr-eraser): ~$200/month at projected load. RabbitMQ StatefulSet: ~$50/month. Grafana/Prometheus: ~$40/month. GCS billing CSV: negligible. Total: ~$430/month, within $600 budget. TimescaleDB chunk compression reduces storage 80% after 30-day compression policy on meter_reads, keeping 7-year retention under 500GB.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (10 weeks): AMQP consumer with manual ACK and ON CONFLICT deduplication, meter_reads TimescaleDB hypertable with UNIQUE constraint, 15-minute consumption interval continuous aggregate, leak-detector service and leak_flags table, field technician leak-flag API, FTP bridge CronJob for parallel run, HPA on RabbitMQ queue depth, Cloud Build CI/CD and GKE Autopilot deployment, GDPR erasure CronJob. Phase 2 (6 weeks): billing-exporter CronJob with daily_consumption_totals continuous aggregate and GCS CSV delivery, operations analytics API for district-level consumption, Grafana billing export dashboard, decommissioning FTP bridge after all concentrators upgraded. Phase 3 (4 weeks): predictive pipe failure scoring using historical leak_flags patterns, GIS district visualization integration, tariff tier automation API.
target_output.development_guidelines: Go 1.22 with golangci-lint in Cloud Build CI. All pipeline service logic (AMQP message schema validation, leak detection query, billing CSV generation) covered by unit tests with mocked TimescaleDB interface. Integration tests in staging replay 24-hour historical read files to validate deduplication and continuous aggregate accuracy. TimescaleDB schema changes (hypertable or continuous aggregate modifications) require explicit migration scripts and ADR. FTP bridge code lives in a separate Go package with feature-flag gate; removal is a planned task when migration is complete. All service_account_id values in log lines masked to last-4 characters by a structured log middleware.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: RabbitMQ single-node StatefulSet is a single point of failure for meter read ingestion. Mitigation: manual ACK with requeue ensures no message loss on broker restart; concentrators buffer locally during outage and replay on reconnect (the same backlog mechanism). Future phase: RabbitMQ cluster for HA. Risk: TimescaleDB chunk-level GDPR erasure performance degrades if service_account_id is not indexed in meter_reads chunks -- a partial index on meter_reads (service_account_id) is required. Added to schema. Risk: FTP bridge parallel run period creates operational complexity if FTP and AMQP feeds diverge (missed reads). Mitigation: daily reconciliation query comparing FTP-sourced vs AMQP-sourced read counts per meter per day, alerting on >5% divergence. Tradeoff: monthly-to-daily billing cycle change requires ERP integration testing with billing system team -- data pipeline delivers the data; ERP readiness is an external dependency.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: ISS-001 resolved: RabbitMQ queue depth HPA for amqp-consumer with 5000-message target; scales to 6 replicas in 3 minutes during 18-hour backlog reconnect. ISS-002 partially addressed: GDPR erasure CronJob designed with chunk scanning, 30-day SLA, and Grafana alert; full testing in staging required before production. ISS-003 resolved: UNIQUE (meter_id, read_at) with ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING makes FTP and AMQP parallel ingestion idempotent; billing totals derived from continuous aggregate are deduplication-safe. Confirmed RabbitMQ over GCP Pub/Sub for AMQP protocol compatibility with meter concentrator firmware. Selected TimescaleDB continuous aggregate over scheduled batch job for 15-minute intervals to reduce operational overhead.

metadata.notes: Municipal water meter AMQP modernization; round 2 adds backlog burst HPA and FTP parallel-run deduplication via ON CONFLICT.

reasoner_reviews.product: The relaunch scope is clearly bounded: highlights, compatibility widget, and pre-order form. The static JSON lookup for compatibility avoids any runtime API dependency and keeps the page fully cacheable at the edge. Ensure the compatibility dataset covers all 12 door types listed in the product brief.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Next.js 14 with SSG is correct here. All product content should be statically generated from MDX files; only the pre-order form route needs a serverless function. The compatibility widget can be a pure client-side component hydrating from a bundled JSON file, keeping edge cache hit rate at 100% for all product pages.
reasoner_reviews.security: Round 1 omitted CSP directive values. The pre-order endpoint must enumerate CSP headers in next.config covering script-src, img-src, connect-src domains for Vercel Analytics and HubSpot. Honeypot field must be validated server-side, not just client-side.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: Lighthouse 95 on mobile is achievable with next/image for all product photography, deferred hydration for below-fold components, and Vercel Edge caching. Three-week timeline is feasible with SSG and a single serverless route.
reasoner_reviews.critic: Round 1 plan omitted explicit CSP directive values and did not define the pre-order serverless function error handling path for HubSpot webhook failures. Both must be addressed in round 2.

frontend.app_structure: Next.js 14 app router with SSG; route segments: / (hero + highlights), /compatibility (checker widget), /pre-order (form). MDX-sourced content for feature highlight cards.
frontend.pages_and_flows: Hero with product lifestyle imagery, Feature Highlights grid (auto-unlock, tamper alerts, fingerprint reader, companion app), Compatibility Checker widget, Pre-order form, FAQ accordion with 8 Q&A entries, Footer with dealer inquiry link.
frontend.performance_notes: next/image with AVIF/WebP for all product photography. Font subsetting for brand typeface. Lazy hydration for FAQ accordion and below-fold sections. Preload LCP hero image. Target LCP < 2.0s on 4G mobile.
frontend.frontend_risks: Compatibility JSON growing large if door SKUs expand beyond 200 entries; mitigate by splitting into per-door-type JSON chunks loaded on demand via dynamic import.

security.auth_design: No authentication required; fully public page.
security.abuse_prevention: Honeypot hidden field 'website_url' on pre-order form validated server-side. Vercel Firewall rule: 5 POST submissions per IP per hour on /api/pre-order. CSP header blocks eval and inline scripts.
security.privacy_controls: Cookie consent banner (shadcn Dialog) gates Vercel Analytics tracking cookie for EU visitors. No third-party pixels loaded without consent signal.

data.entities: product_variants stored as variants.json (id, name, sku, color_hex, price_usd, available_at). feature_highlights as MDX files in /content/features. compatibility_entries as compatibility.json (door_type, deadbolt_brand, hand, compatible BOOL, adapter_sku, notes). pre_order_leads captured externally in HubSpot only.
data.storage_design: All content in repository as MDX and JSON files; no database. Compatibility lookup is bundled client-side at build time.
data.retention_and_deletion: No on-site data retained; HubSpot governs lead data lifecycle under its DPA.

devops.deployment_topology: Vercel Edge Network serves pre-built HTML/CSS/JS globally. Single Vercel Serverless Function for /api/pre-order in us-east-1.
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: eslint, tsc --noEmit, next build on every PR. Vercel GitHub integration creates preview URL per PR. Merge to main triggers production deploy completing in ~90 seconds.
devops.observability_stack: Vercel Analytics for Core Web Vitals per route. Custom GTM dataLayer events: compatibility_checked, preorder_submitted, preorder_error. Vercel Log Drain for /api/pre-order function errors.
devops.devops_risks: HubSpot webhook downtime would drop leads silently; mitigate by logging failed payloads to Vercel log drain for manual recovery and alerting on error rate > 5% over 10-minute window.

target_output.title: Smart Lock Relaunch Landing Page
target_output.executive_summary: A statically generated Next.js 14 landing page for the consumer smart door lock relaunch, served globally via Vercel Edge Network. All product content is SSG-rendered from MDX source files with zero runtime backend. The client-side compatibility checker resolves door type and deadbolt brand combinations from a bundled JSON lookup. A single Vercel serverless function handles pre-order submissions, forwarding lead data to HubSpot with explicit error handling, rate-limit protection, and full CSP coverage.
target_output.architecture_overview: Static-first architecture: Next.js 14 app router generates all product pages at build time using MDX content files, producing fully cacheable HTML served from Vercel Edge PoPs globally. The door compatibility checker is a React component hydrating client-side from a bundled compatibility.json (covering 3 door types, 12 deadbolt brands, 2 hand orientations). One serverless function at /api/pre-order handles form POSTs, validates input with Zod, posts to the HubSpot Forms API v3, and returns structured success or error responses with retry guidance. No database, session state, or persistent backend exists on-site; all lead data lives in HubSpot CRM governed by its DPA.
target_output.technology_stack: Frontend: Next.js 14 (app router, SSG), TypeScript strict mode, Tailwind CSS, shadcn/ui component library. Content authoring: MDX for feature highlight cards, YAML for product variant metadata, JSON for compatibility lookup. Serverless: Next.js API Route on Vercel Functions (Node.js runtime, 128 MB, 10s timeout). Input validation: Zod schema for pre-order form. CDN: Vercel Edge Network. Analytics: Vercel Analytics (Core Web Vitals), Vercel Speed Insights. Lead capture: HubSpot Forms API v3. CI: GitHub Actions with ESLint, tsc --noEmit, next build.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Hero section with product lifestyle photography (AVIF/WebP) and primary CTA. Feature Highlights grid: auto-unlock via phone geofence, tamper alarm with push notification within 5 seconds, fingerprint plus keypad dual-mode entry, companion iOS/Android app with remote lock history. Door Compatibility Checker: user selects door thickness (1-3/8 inch / 1-3/4 inch / 2 inch), hand orientation, and existing deadbolt brand from dropdowns; widget returns pass/fail badge and recommended installation kit SKU. Pre-order form: name, email, shipping country, preferred finish color (matte black, brushed nickel, brass). FAQ accordion: 8 questions covering installation time, battery life, Z-Wave compatibility, and guest access codes. Footer with dealer inquiry email link.
target_output.system_components: Vercel Edge CDN serving pre-built HTML/CSS/JS for all product content routes. Next.js app router with generateStaticParams for feature and FAQ segments. Client-side CompatibilityChecker React component bundled with compatibility.json (under 15 KB gzipped). Vercel Serverless Function /api/pre-order (us-east-1 region). HubSpot Forms API v3 as external lead capture sink. GitHub Actions CI pipeline. Vercel Log Drain for function error capture.
target_output.workflows: Compatibility Check Flow: User selects door thickness, deadbolt brand (Schlage, Kwikset, Yale, Baldwin, generic), and hand from three dropdowns. React component reads in-memory compatibility lookup synchronously and renders within 50 ms: green 'Compatible - No Adapter Needed' badge with recommended installation kit SKU, or yellow 'Adapter Kit Required' badge with accessory product link. No network call is made. Pre-order Submission Flow: User fills name, email, country, preferred finish and clicks 'Reserve Now'. Client-side Zod validation highlights any missing required fields. On valid submit, POST to /api/pre-order. Server re-validates payload, calls HubSpot Forms API POST to /submissions/v3/integration/submit/{portalId}/{formGuid}. On 200, returns JSON {success: true, ref_id} and client shows toast 'You are on the list - check your email'. On HubSpot 4xx/5xx, server logs full error via console.error (captured by Vercel Log Drain), returns 502 {error: 'upstream_failure', retry_after: 30}, and client displays toast 'Submission failed - please retry in 30 seconds or email preorder@brand.com'.
target_output.data_model: product_variants (id UUID, name TEXT, sku TEXT, color_hex TEXT, price_usd DECIMAL, available_at DATE) - committed as /data/variants.json, 3 finish variants. feature_highlights (id TEXT, title TEXT, icon_slug TEXT, body_md TEXT, display_order INT) - MDX files in /content/features, one file per feature. compatibility_entries (door_type ENUM '1-3/8in'/'1-3/4in'/'2in', deadbolt_brand TEXT, hand ENUM 'left'/'right'/'reversible', compatible BOOL, adapter_sku TEXT nullable, notes TEXT) - /data/compatibility.json, 72 rows. pre_order_leads - not stored on-site; HubSpot contact record with custom properties preferred_finish and shipping_country.
target_output.api_design: POST /api/pre-order - accepts JSON {first_name: string, last_name: string, email: string, shipping_country: string ISO-3166-1 alpha-2, preferred_finish: 'matte_black'|'brushed_nickel'|'brass'}. Zod schema validates all fields. Honeypot field 'website_url' must be empty or request is discarded with 200 (anti-spam silent accept). On valid payload, forwards to HubSpot Forms API. Returns: 200 {success: true, ref_id: string} on success; 400 {error: 'validation', fields: string[]} on schema failure; 429 {error: 'rate_limited', retry_after: 60} when Vercel Firewall blocks; 502 {error: 'upstream_failure', retry_after: 30} on HubSpot error.
target_output.security_and_compliance: HTTPS enforced by Vercel automatic TLS. Content-Security-Policy configured in next.config headers: default-src 'self'; script-src 'self' https://vercel.live https://www.googletagmanager.com; style-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline'; img-src 'self' data: https://cdn.brand.com; connect-src 'self' https://api.hsforms.com https://vitals.vercel-insights.com; frame-ancestors 'none'. X-Frame-Options: DENY. Referrer-Policy: strict-origin-when-cross-origin. Honeypot field 'website_url' (CSS display:none) validated server-side; non-empty value discards submission silently. Vercel Firewall rule limits /api/pre-order to 5 POST per IP per hour. Cookie consent banner (shadcn Dialog) gates Vercel Analytics cookie for EU visitors per GDPR. No session tokens, no auth cookies, no PII stored on-site.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: GitHub Actions CI runs on every PR: eslint --max-warnings 0, tsc --noEmit, next build. Build failure blocks merge. Vercel GitHub integration creates a preview deployment URL per PR for stakeholder review, available within 2 minutes of push. All environment variables (HUBSPOT_PORTAL_ID, HUBSPOT_FORM_GUID) stored in Vercel project settings, never in repository. Merge to main triggers Vercel production deploy completing in approximately 90 seconds. Cache-Control: public, max-age=31536000, immutable on all hashed static assets. Content updates (MDX files, compatibility JSON) go through PR review and CI before merging.
target_output.observability: Vercel Analytics dashboard tracks Core Web Vitals (LCP, CLS, FID) per route with P75 and P95 segmented by device type. Target: LCP < 2.0s on mobile P75. Vercel Speed Insights provides real-user performance waterfall traces. Custom GTM dataLayer events: compatibility_checked (with door_type and result properties), preorder_form_started, preorder_submitted (with preferred_finish), preorder_error (with error_type). Vercel Log Drain captures /api/pre-order function logs; alert via Vercel webhook to Slack on error rate > 5% over 10-minute window.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Vercel Pro plan at $20/month covers static hosting and serverless function invocations for under 50K monthly visits. All product content pages served from edge cache at zero marginal compute cost. Compatibility checker has zero per-request cost (bundled client-side). HubSpot Free CRM tier sufficient for pre-launch lead capture. Total estimated monthly cost under $25.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (3 weeks): Hero, feature highlights, compatibility checker, pre-order form with HubSpot integration, full CSP header configuration, rate limiting, cookie consent banner. Deploy to Vercel production. Gate go-live on Lighthouse mobile score >= 95. Phase 2 (4 weeks post-launch): Dealer locator page with Leaflet map reading /data/dealers.json. Brand story video section with next/dynamic lazy load. Hreflang tags and locale routing for EU and APAC markets. Phase 3 - Conversion optimization: A/B test hero CTA copy via Vercel Edge Middleware flags. Exit-intent pre-order modal. Hotjar heatmaps with CSP nonce for session recording.
target_output.development_guidelines: TypeScript strict mode with no-any rule enforced. ESLint next/core-web-vitals ruleset; zero warnings in CI. Compatibility JSON edits require accompanying Jest test covering each new door-brand-hand combination. All new UI sections require Lighthouse accessibility score >= 90. MDX content changes require product owner approval in PR. Single engineering review required before merge to main.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: HubSpot Forms API outage drops pre-order leads without on-site backup. Mitigation: /api/pre-order logs full request payload on failure via console.error to Vercel Log Drain; team alert on error rate spike enables manual recovery. Risk: Compatibility JSON growing beyond 50 KB as lock SKU line expands. Mitigation: split into per-door-type JSON chunks loaded via dynamic import on checker interaction. Tradeoff: Next.js 14 chosen over pure Astro for team familiarity, accepting slightly larger JS bundle; mitigated by aggressive code splitting and deferred hydration for all below-fold content.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Confirmed HubSpot as CRM target over Mailchimp based on existing sales team contract. Confirmed static JSON for compatibility checker over headless CMS API to eliminate runtime dependency and maximize edge cache hit rate. Confirmed Vercel Firewall for rate limiting over Cloudflare WAF to maintain single-vendor deployment stack simplicity.
