reasoner_reviews.product: The documentation content types are well-defined: quickstart, API reference, benchmark tables, and model conversion tutorials. The benchmark table approach - reading versioned JSON at build time - ensures published numbers always match tested firmware. The round 1 plan was thin on the benchmark data pipeline and lacked specifics about versioned doc structure.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Docusaurus 3 is the correct choice for a library documentation site of this nature. Benchmark table generation via a build-time script reading JSON files is the right pattern. The API reference should be auto-generated from source annotations to stay in sync with library releases. Round 1 did not specify the versioning strategy for docs across library releases.
reasoner_reviews.security: Static site on GitHub Pages with HTTPS is low risk. Subresource integrity should be applied to any third-party CDN assets. Plausible Analytics script should use the self-hosted option or the official CDN with SRI hash to prevent supply-chain issues.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: GitHub Actions free tier allows 2000 minutes/month; a Docusaurus build for this site should complete in under 2 minutes, well within budget. Zero hosting cost is achievable on GitHub Pages.

frontend.app_structure: Docusaurus 3 site with /docs (API reference and tutorials), /benchmarks (comparison tables page), /blog (release notes). Custom React components for benchmark comparison tables and MCU compatibility matrix.
frontend.pages_and_flows: Landing page with hero, library feature highlights, and quickstart snippet. Docs sidebar with versioned navigation. Benchmark page with filterable table by MCU board and model type. Tutorial pages with syntax-highlighted code blocks for C++ model conversion steps.
frontend.performance_notes: Docusaurus static output is pre-rendered; no client hydration on docs pages. Code blocks use Prism.js for syntax highlighting of C++ and Python. All images in WebP format. Algolia DocSearch for instant client-side search.
frontend.frontend_risks: API reference auto-generation requires source JSDoc to be consistently maintained by contributors; gaps will produce incomplete reference pages.

security.auth_design: No authentication; fully public.
security.abuse_prevention: No user input forms. GitHub Pages enforces HTTPS. Plausible Analytics loaded from official CDN with SRI hash pinned in Docusaurus config.
security.privacy_controls: Plausible Analytics is cookieless and GDPR-exempt; no consent banner required. No third-party tracking scripts.

data.storage_design: All content stored as files in repository. Benchmark JSON read at build time by custom Docusaurus plugin generating table components.
data.retention_and_deletion: Repository git history; no external data store.

devops.deployment_topology: GitHub Actions workflow on push to main: docusaurus build, output to /out, deploy to gh-pages branch. Custom domain via CNAME file.
devops.ci_cd_design: PR checks: eslint, TypeScript typecheck, docusaurus build --dry-run. Merge to main triggers full build and deploy. Build caches node_modules via actions/cache for sub-2-minute builds.
devops.observability_stack: Plausible Analytics script in Docusaurus head for cookieless pageviews by path. No server-side logging.
devops.devops_risks: GitHub Pages downtime (rare) affects all documentation access; no fallback CDN configured in round 1. Consider Cloudflare proxy in future.

target_output.title: Edge ML Inference Library Documentation Site
target_output.executive_summary: A static Docusaurus 3 documentation and showcase site for an open-source edge ML inference library targeting microcontrollers, deployed to GitHub Pages at zero hosting cost. API reference pages are auto-generated from library source JSDoc annotations. Benchmark comparison tables are built at deploy time from versioned JSON result files, ensuring published latency and memory numbers always reflect the tested firmware release. Documentation is versioned per library release using Docusaurus native versioning.
target_output.architecture_overview: Fully static Docusaurus 3 site with no server-side runtime. Content lives in the library repository under /website. Three content sources feed the build: Markdown and MDX files for tutorials and guides, JSDoc annotations in library source code processed by a Docusaurus API plugin into reference pages, and versioned JSON benchmark files in /benchmarks/data processed by a custom Remark plugin into comparison table components. GitHub Actions runs the build on every merge to main and deploys the output to the gh-pages branch, which GitHub Pages serves over HTTPS with a custom domain.
target_output.technology_stack: Site generator: Docusaurus 3 with React 18. Content: MDX for tutorials and guides, JSDoc-to-docs plugin for C++ API reference. Benchmark rendering: custom Docusaurus plugin reading /benchmarks/data/{version}.json and generating React table components. Search: Algolia DocSearch (free tier for OSS). Analytics: Plausible Analytics (cookieless, self-hosted script). Syntax highlighting: Prism.js with C++, Python, and bash grammars. CI/CD: GitHub Actions with actions/cache for node_modules. Hosting: GitHub Pages with CNAME custom domain.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Quickstart guide: install via pip/CMake, load a TFLite model, run inference on Arduino Nano 33 BLE in 15 lines of C++. API reference: auto-generated from library source for Model, Tensor, Interpreter, and QuantizationConfig classes. Benchmark tables: filterable by MCU board (Arduino Nano, STM32F4, ESP32-S3, RP2040) and model type (MobileNetV1 INT8, keyword spotting, anomaly detection); columns for latency ms, flash KB, RAM KB, firmware version. Model conversion tutorial: step-by-step Keras to TFLite to library-native format with per-layer quantization options. Changelog page: auto-generated from CHANGELOG.md per library version.
target_output.system_components: Docusaurus 3 build process consuming MDX content, JSDoc plugin output, and benchmark JSON plugin output. Custom BenchmarkTable React component rendering filterable MCU performance data. Algolia DocSearch crawler indexing published docs weekly. Plausible Analytics script tag in Docusaurus head. GitHub Actions CI/CD workflow. GitHub Pages serving static output with HTTPS and custom domain via CNAME.
target_output.workflows: Content Update Workflow: Contributor opens PR with updated Markdown or MDX file in /website/docs. GitHub Actions runs eslint and docusaurus build --dry-run as a PR check. Reviewer approves; merge to main triggers full build and deploy to gh-pages branch, site updated within 3 minutes. Benchmark Publish Workflow: Library maintainer runs the benchmark harness against target MCU boards after each firmware release, producing a JSON file like /benchmarks/data/v1.2.json with columns mcu_board, model_name, latency_ms, flash_kb, ram_kb, firmware_version. Maintainer commits the file to the repository. On next main branch merge, the Docusaurus benchmark plugin reads all versioned JSON files and regenerates the comparison table page with the new release row added.
target_output.data_model: doc_pages: MDX files in /website/docs organized by category (getting-started, api, tutorials, advanced). api_references: auto-generated at build time from library C++ headers via doxygen-to-markdown pipeline; output cached in /website/docs/api. benchmark_results: JSON files in /benchmarks/data/{version}.json - each file is an array of objects with fields mcu_board (string), model_name (string), latency_ms (number), flash_kb (number), ram_kb (number), firmware_version (string), tested_at (ISO date). tutorials: MDX files in /website/docs/tutorials with embedded C++ and Python code examples. code_examples: inline MDX code blocks with language tags for Prism.js highlighting.
target_output.api_design: No runtime API. Build-time data access only: GET /benchmarks/data/{version}.json served as static asset. Algolia DocSearch API called client-side for search queries against the weekly-indexed search index. Plausible Analytics pageview events sent client-side via plausible() function call. No server endpoints.
target_output.security_and_compliance: HTTPS enforced by GitHub Pages for all traffic including custom domain. Content Security Policy meta tag in Docusaurus head: default-src 'self'; script-src 'self' https://plausible.io; style-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline'; img-src 'self' data:; connect-src 'self' https://plausible.io https://{algolia-app-id}-dsn.algolia.net. Subresource integrity hash applied to Plausible Analytics script tag. No inline scripts in content. No cookies set by the site; Plausible is cookieless. GitHub-managed repository access controls protect the gh-pages branch from unauthorized writes.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: GitHub Actions workflow .github/workflows/deploy.yml triggered on push to main. Steps: checkout, setup-node 20, restore node_modules cache (actions/cache keyed on package-lock.json hash), npm install if cache miss, docusaurus build (output to /build), deploy to gh-pages branch via peaceiris/actions-gh-pages action with CNAME file. Expected build time: under 2 minutes with warm cache, under 3 minutes cold. PR checks: eslint --max-warnings 0, tsc --noEmit, docusaurus build --dry-run. Deployment to GitHub Pages completes within 60 seconds of gh-pages branch update.
target_output.observability: Plausible Analytics tracks cookieless pageviews by URL path, referrer source, and device type. Key metrics: weekly active users, most-visited doc sections (quickstart vs API reference vs benchmarks), search query terms via Algolia DocSearch analytics dashboard. No server-side metrics available for static hosting. Contributors monitor PR CI build times to detect dependency bloat.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Zero hosting cost on GitHub Pages for OSS repositories. GitHub Actions free tier provides 2000 minutes/month; estimated 1.5 minutes per deploy means up to 1300 deploys/month before billing. Algolia DocSearch is free for OSS projects. Plausible Analytics OSS self-hosted or free tier for low-traffic projects. Total cost: $0 for all components at expected traffic volumes.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 Launch (2 weeks): Quickstart guide, API reference for core classes, benchmark table for 3 MCU boards (Arduino Nano 33 BLE, ESP32-S3, RP2040), model conversion tutorial, Docusaurus versioning configured for v1.x as latest stable, GitHub Actions CI/CD pipeline. Phase 2 (ongoing with releases): Add new MCU benchmark rows per firmware release, expand API reference as library surface grows, add advanced tutorials for custom model quantization and RTOS integration. Phase 3 (post v2.0): Interactive WASM inference demo embedded in docs page, multi-version dropdown showing v1.x and v2.x docs concurrently.
target_output.development_guidelines: All documentation changes via GitHub PR with one maintainer review. Benchmark JSON files must include firmware_version and tested_at fields to be accepted by the plugin schema validation. JSDoc annotations in library source are required for all public API classes and methods; CI fails if undocumented public symbols are detected. MDX code examples must specify language tag for Prism.js highlighting. Node.js version pinned in .nvmrc for consistent local builds.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: JSDoc plugin falls out of sync if library contributors skip annotation updates. Mitigation: CI check verifies all public symbols have JSDoc before merge to main. Risk: Algolia DocSearch crawler lag means new content is not searchable for up to 24 hours after publish. Mitigation: document this limitation in the contributor guide; acceptable for OSS docs. Tradeoff: Docusaurus over pure Astro chosen for native versioning and OSS ecosystem adoption; Astro would yield smaller bundles but lacks built-in docs versioning.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Confirmed Docusaurus native versioning over a custom versioning script for lower maintenance burden. Confirmed Plausible Analytics over Google Analytics to avoid cookie consent banner requirement. Confirmed GitHub Pages over Netlify to eliminate any hosting cost for the OSS project.

project.goal: Zigbee-to-Matter protocol migration research prototype for lab experiments

backend.protocol_interfaces: python-matter-server for Matter, zigpy for Zigbee

security.data_access: SQLite file permissions restricted to research user

data.monitoring: Prometheus metrics exposed on :9090, Grafana dashboard on :3000

devops.ci: GitHub Actions for unit tests and lint
devops.deploy: Git pull and systemd service restart

reasoner_reviews.product: The experiment scope is well-defined for academic research: 30-minute soak periods, per-message RTT logging, topology configuration files. The round 1 plan was thin on how topology configurations are defined and how the experiment runner transitions between topologies without manual intervention.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Flask is appropriate for a local experiment control API. The key architectural gap from round 1 is the topology configuration schema - researchers need to define network topologies declaratively as YAML/JSON files and have the experiment runner apply them programmatically. The Prometheus exporter should push live latency percentiles during soak periods.
reasoner_reviews.security: Local-only deployment with SSH key access is appropriate for this risk level. SQLite file permissions and no network exposure are sufficient controls.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: Raspberry Pi 5 ARM64 with Python 3.12 is well-supported by python-matter-server and zigpy. The 2 GB SQLite limit is achievable for 30-minute soak periods at 1 message/second per device across 20 test devices.

backend.service_design: Flask app with four modules: experiment_runner (orchestrates run lifecycle), topology_manager (applies network configs via python-matter-server and zigpy), metrics_collector (captures RTT and packet loss per message), report_generator (exports CSV and aggregate stats).
backend.api_patterns: Local REST API on :5000: POST /experiments/start, POST /experiments/stop, GET /experiments/{run_id}/status, GET /experiments/{run_id}/report. No auth; local network only.
backend.background_jobs: Soak period message loop runs as a background thread; sends test messages to each device every second and records RTT in SQLite. Prometheus metrics updated every 5 seconds from in-memory counters.
backend.failure_handling: Device pairing failures logged with error code and retry count; experiment run aborted if more than 20% of devices fail pairing. SQLite writes use WAL mode to prevent corruption on Raspberry Pi power loss.

security.auth_design: No application-level auth; access via SSH key to Raspberry Pi only.
security.secrets_management: No secrets required; all credentials are SSH key-based for local hardware.
security.audit_and_logging_controls: Structured JSON logs per experiment run written to /var/log/protocol-research/ with run_id, topology_id, timestamp, event_type.

data.entities: test_devices (id, device_eui, protocol ENUM zigbee/matter, model, firmware_version, paired_at). protocol_sessions (id, run_id, device_id, protocol, session_start, session_end, messages_sent, messages_received). latency_samples (id, session_id, seq_num, sent_at, received_at, rtt_ms, lost BOOL). pairing_attempts (id, run_id, device_id, protocol, attempt_num, success BOOL, error_code, duration_ms). topology_configs (id, name, description, config_json). experiment_runs (id, topology_id, protocol, start_time, end_time, status ENUM running/completed/aborted, notes).
data.storage_design: SQLite database /data/experiments.db in WAL mode. Prometheus time-series in-memory only during run; not persisted. CSV exports written to /data/reports/{run_id}.csv.
data.migration_notes: Schema migrations managed with Alembic; migration scripts run on startup if pending.
data.data_risks: SQLite WAL mode does not guarantee durability on power loss; Raspberry Pi should use UPS or researchers should commit to graceful shutdown before power off.

devops.deployment_topology: Single Raspberry Pi 5 running Flask app as systemd service (protocol-research.service). Prometheus exporter on :9090. Grafana on :3000 reading Prometheus.
devops.environments: Single environment: lab on-premises. No staging.
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: pytest unit tests and flake8 lint on every PR. Deploy by SSH into Raspberry Pi, git pull, pip install -r requirements.txt, systemctl restart protocol-research.
devops.rollback_strategy: Git revert to previous commit and redeploy. SQLite backup before each experiment run to /data/backups/.

target_output.title: Zigbee-to-Matter Protocol Migration Research Prototype
target_output.executive_summary: A Python Flask-based research prototype running on a Raspberry Pi 5 in a lab apartment environment to measure Zigbee-to-Matter protocol migration impact on latency, packet loss, and device pairing reliability. Experiment runs are defined by declarative YAML topology configuration files; the runner pairs all devices, executes a 30-minute soak period logging per-message round-trip times to SQLite, and generates comparative CSV reports. Prometheus and Grafana provide live telemetry during soak periods for anomaly detection.
target_output.architecture_overview: Single-host architecture on Raspberry Pi 5 ARM64. A Flask application serves a local REST experiment control API on port 5000. The experiment runner module reads a YAML topology configuration, applies it to the device network via python-matter-server (for Matter) or zigpy (for Zigbee), pairs all test devices, and starts the soak period message loop as a background thread. Per-message RTT and packet loss data is written to SQLite in WAL mode. A Prometheus exporter pushes live latency percentiles and packet loss rate to a Prometheus instance on port 9090, visualized by Grafana on port 3000. At run completion, the report generator aggregates results by topology and protocol version, producing CSV files for statistical analysis in Jupyter notebooks.
target_output.technology_stack: Backend: Python 3.12, Flask 3, SQLAlchemy 2 with SQLite WAL mode. Protocol libraries: python-matter-server for Matter commissioning and messaging, zigpy with zigpy-znp adapter for Zigbee. Experiment config: PyYAML for topology configuration file parsing, Pydantic for config schema validation. Metrics: Prometheus Python client for live exporter, Grafana 10 for dashboards. Analysis: Jupyter Notebooks with pandas and matplotlib for post-experiment CSV analysis. Database migrations: Alembic. CI: GitHub Actions with pytest and flake8.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Topology Configuration Loader: reads YAML files from /configs/topologies/, validates against Pydantic schema, registers device list and network parameters. Experiment Runner: orchestrates pairing of all topology devices, starts soak period message loop (1 message/second/device for 1800 seconds), writes results to SQLite, exposes run status via REST API. Metrics Exporter: computes rolling p50/p95/p99 latency and packet loss rate every 5 seconds, pushes to Prometheus for Grafana visualization. Report Generator: aggregates latency statistics (mean, stddev, p99, loss rate) per device and topology from SQLite, exports to /data/reports/{run_id}.csv. Pairing Reliability Tracker: logs each pairing attempt result (success/failure, duration, error code) for pairing success rate analysis across protocols.
target_output.system_components: Flask REST API (port 5000) accepting experiment control commands. Experiment Runner background thread managing soak period message loops. python-matter-server daemon managing Matter fabric and device commissioning (port 5580). zigpy-znp controller connected to USB Zigbee coordinator dongle. SQLite database /data/experiments.db in WAL mode. Prometheus client exporting metrics on port 9090. Grafana instance on port 3000 with pre-built soak period dashboard. Alembic migration runner executing on Flask startup.
target_output.workflows: Experiment Run Lifecycle: Researcher creates a topology config YAML in /configs/topologies/. Example config for a Matter star topology: topology_name: matter_star_5dev, protocol: matter, channel: 25, network_key_mode: auto, devices: [{eui: AA:BB:CC:DD:EE:01, role: border_router}, {eui: AA:BB:CC:DD:EE:02, role: end_device}, ...]. Researcher calls POST /experiments/start with {topology_id, duration_seconds: 1800, notes: 'Run 3 channel 25'}. The runner loads the YAML, validates with Pydantic, calls python-matter-server commission_device() for each Matter device or zigpy form_network() and join_network() for each Zigbee device. On pairing completion (or partial failure logged), the 1800-second soak loop starts: send a ping command to each device sequentially, record sent_at and received_at timestamps, write latency_samples row to SQLite. Prometheus metrics update every 5 seconds. On loop completion, runner sets run status to completed. Report Export Workflow: Researcher calls GET /experiments/{run_id}/report which triggers the report generator to query SQLite, compute per-device and aggregate statistics, and write /data/reports/{run_id}.csv. Researcher copies CSV to workstation and opens in Jupyter Notebook for statistical comparison across topologies.
target_output.data_model: test_devices (id INT PK, device_eui TEXT UNIQUE, protocol ENUM 'zigbee'/'matter', model TEXT, firmware_version TEXT, paired_at DATETIME). protocol_sessions (id INT PK, run_id INT FK, device_id INT FK, protocol TEXT, session_start DATETIME, session_end DATETIME, messages_sent INT, messages_received INT). latency_samples (id INT PK, session_id INT FK, seq_num INT, sent_at DATETIME, received_at DATETIME NULLABLE, rtt_ms REAL NULLABLE, lost BOOL). pairing_attempts (id INT PK, run_id INT FK, device_id INT FK, protocol TEXT, attempt_num INT, success BOOL, error_code TEXT NULLABLE, duration_ms INT). topology_configs (id INT PK, name TEXT UNIQUE, description TEXT, config_json TEXT). experiment_runs (id INT PK, topology_id INT FK, protocol TEXT, start_time DATETIME, end_time DATETIME NULLABLE, status ENUM 'running'/'completed'/'aborted', notes TEXT). Topology YAML schema (Pydantic model): topology_name (str), protocol (Literal['zigbee','matter']), channel (int 11-26), network_key_mode (Literal['auto','manual']), devices (list of {eui: str, role: Literal['coordinator','router','border_router','end_device'], model: str optional}).
target_output.api_design: POST /experiments/start - body {topology_id: int, duration_seconds: int, notes: str optional}. Returns {run_id, status: 'running', started_at}. POST /experiments/stop - body {run_id: int}. Returns {run_id, status: 'aborted', stopped_at}. GET /experiments/{run_id}/status - returns {run_id, status, elapsed_seconds, messages_sent, messages_received, current_loss_rate_pct, p99_latency_ms}. GET /experiments/{run_id}/report - triggers CSV generation and returns {report_path, summary: {total_messages, loss_rate_pct, mean_rtt_ms, p99_rtt_ms}}. GET /topologies - lists available topology config files. All endpoints local network only; no authentication.
target_output.security_and_compliance: Local network access only; Flask app bound to 127.0.0.1 or LAN interface, not 0.0.0.0. SSH key authentication for Raspberry Pi access; password login disabled. SQLite database file permissions set to 600 (owner read/write only, research user). Experiment log files in /var/log/protocol-research/ owned by research user. No secrets or credentials stored in code; python-matter-server uses auto-generated Matter fabric credentials stored in its local state directory. Network key for Zigbee stored in zigpy state file with restricted permissions.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: Application runs as a systemd service (protocol-research.service) on Raspberry Pi 5 starting on boot. Service definition configures WorkingDirectory, User=research, Restart=on-failure. Deploy procedure: SSH into Raspberry Pi, git pull origin main, pip install -r requirements.txt (offline-capable with pre-cached wheels), alembic upgrade head, systemctl restart protocol-research. Grafana and Prometheus run as additional systemd services installed via apt. SQLite backup created automatically by a cron job before each experiment run: cp /data/experiments.db /data/backups/experiments_$(date +%Y%m%d_%H%M%S).db. GitHub Actions CI runs pytest and flake8 on every PR; no automated deployment from CI (manual deploy via SSH for lab safety).
target_output.observability: Prometheus Python client exposes custom metrics on port 9090: protocol_rtt_p50_ms, protocol_rtt_p95_ms, protocol_rtt_p99_ms, protocol_packet_loss_rate (all labeled by run_id, protocol, topology_name). Grafana dashboard 'Soak Period Monitor' plots all four metrics in real time during experiment runs. Structured JSON logs written per event to /var/log/protocol-research/run_{run_id}.log with fields: timestamp, event_type (pairing_success, pairing_failure, message_sent, message_received, run_completed, run_aborted), device_eui, rtt_ms, error_code. Researchers monitor Grafana during soak periods and abort runs showing > 30% packet loss as anomalous.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Zero cloud cost; all components run on the existing Raspberry Pi 5 lab hardware. SQLite handles up to 20 devices at 1 message/second over 30 minutes, generating approximately 36,000 latency_samples rows per run (under 50 MB). At 10 runs per topology configuration, total DB size stays well within the 2 GB limit. If experiment scale grows to 100 devices, migrate to PostgreSQL on a more powerful host.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (4 weeks): Topology config YAML loader with Pydantic validation, experiment runner with Matter and Zigbee pairing, 30-minute soak period message loop, SQLite storage, CSV report generator, basic Flask REST API, Prometheus + Grafana dashboard, systemd service deployment. Phase 2 (weeks 5-8): Add multi-topology batch runner executing a predefined sequence of topologies overnight, pairing reliability analysis dashboard in Grafana, automated CSV comparison report across protocol versions. Phase 3 (post-paper submission): Thread protocol comparison extension, GPIO relay control for automated router power cycling during topology transitions.
target_output.development_guidelines: Python 3.12 with type annotations on all function signatures. flake8 and mypy in CI with zero warnings. pytest coverage >= 80% for experiment_runner and report_generator modules. Topology config YAML files committed to /configs/topologies/ with a descriptive name matching topology_name field. Alembic migration required for any schema change; no raw ALTER TABLE statements. Researchers add new topology configs without code review if schema validates; code changes require PR review.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Raspberry Pi power loss during soak period corrupts SQLite WAL file. Mitigation: WAL mode provides crash recovery; pre-run backup cron job enables restore from last checkpoint. Risk: python-matter-server version updates break pairing API compatibility. Mitigation: pin python-matter-server version in requirements.txt and test upgrades before applying to lab Pi. Tradeoff: SQLite over InfluxDB chosen to minimize dependencies and work offline; acceptable given lab-scale data volumes and infrequent concurrent writes.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Confirmed SQLite over InfluxDB for simplicity and offline-capable deployment. Confirmed YAML topology configs over database-stored configs for easy version-control and researcher self-service. Confirmed Prometheus + Grafana over a custom dashboard given team familiarity and zero-cost local deployment.

metadata.notes: Round 2 revision of Zigbee-to-Matter protocol migration research prototype; addresses topology config schema gap from round 1.

profile.domain: University Research - Indoor Air Quality IoT

project_goal.value: Research prototype aggregating multi-modal sensor streams from a campus-wide IoT grid to study indoor air quality correlation with cognitive performance outcomes
project_goal.rationale: Core academic research objective for university grant project

target_users.value: Researchers and data scientists at the university analyzing IAQ and cognition data; IoT infrastructure team managing sensor nodes
target_users.rationale: Internal research team and supporting IT staff

project_class.rationale: Research tool for data collection and analysis, not production SaaS

capabilities.rationale: Sensor ingestion, time-series storage, query API, and MQTT integration; no frontend needed for researcher workflow

complexity_level.rationale: Multi-modal sensor fusion, IRB-compliant anonymization pipeline, and Parquet export for HPC analysis add significant complexity

risk_level.rationale: Participant behavioral data (occupancy, study session links) constitutes personal data under GDPR and requires IRB-compliant handling

data_sensitivity.rationale: Participant session records linked to anonymized cognitive test scores are personal data under GDPR

external_exposure.rationale: University internal network only; no public internet exposure

access_model.value: Keycloak OIDC with researcher and admin roles; API key auth for sensor nodes
access_model.rationale: Researcher role can query and export data; admin role manages sensor node registration

feature_scope.value: Sensor node registration, MQTT ingestion for CO2/humidity/occupancy/acoustic streams, air quality index computation, data exploration API with time-range queries, Parquet export for HPC analysis, IRB anonymization pipeline for participant data
feature_scope.rationale: Complete feature set for academic publication research workflow

mvp_scope.value: MQTT ingestion for CO2 and occupancy sensors, InfluxDB storage, data exploration API with time-range and room-level queries, CSV/Parquet export
mvp_scope.rationale: Minimum to begin data collection for first study cohort

security_baseline.value: Keycloak OIDC for researcher authentication, TLS for all API traffic, API key per sensor node, InfluxDB access restricted to internal network, participant data anonymized before query exposure
security_baseline.rationale: GDPR and IRB requirements for participant data protection

frontend_stack.value: None; Jupyter Notebooks for analysis, Grafana for operational dashboards
frontend_stack.rationale: Researchers prefer Python-native analysis over custom UI

backend_stack.value: Python 3.12 with FastAPI
backend_stack.rationale: Async FastAPI suitable for concurrent MQTT ingestion and query handling on university bare-metal Kubernetes

data_platform.value: InfluxDB 2 for time-series sensor data; Redis 7 for ingestion rate limiting and short-term aggregation cache
data_platform.rationale: InfluxDB optimized for high-cardinality time-series; Redis for windowed aggregation without repeated InfluxDB scans

hosting_target.value: Bare-metal Kubernetes on university on-premises cluster
hosting_target.rationale: University IT policy requires research data to stay on-premises; no cloud storage for participant data

privacy_retention_policy.value: Sensor data retained for 5 years per university grant policy; participant records anonymized within 90 days of study session completion; anonymized data retained indefinitely for publication
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: University IRB protocol and GDPR data minimization requirements

future_scope.value: Acoustic noise frequency analysis, CO2 predictive alerting via ML model, federated anonymized data sharing with partner universities
future_scope.rationale: Follow-on research directions for multi-university collaboration

constraints.value: Must run on university bare-metal Kubernetes; no cloud services for participant data; InfluxDB bucket retention policies set by IRB timeline; all external publications use anonymized datasets only
constraints.rationale: University IT policy and IRB protocol constraints

observability_baseline.value: Grafana + Prometheus for sensor ingestion rate, InfluxDB query latency, MQTT broker message lag; structured JSON application logs
observability_baseline.rationale: Operational visibility for research infrastructure team

execution_preference.value: Iterative deployment; new sensor node types added via configuration without code changes
execution_preference.rationale: Research team needs to add new sensor modalities quickly as grant scope expands

compliance_context.value: GDPR for participant personal data; university IRB protocol for human subjects research; data anonymization mandated before external publication
compliance_context.rationale: Participant cognitive test data linked to occupancy records constitutes personal data under GDPR

project.goal: Campus IAQ IoT research prototype for cognitive performance correlation study
project.timeline: 8 weeks to first data collection cohort

backend.api: REST with OpenAPI 3.0

security.auth: Keycloak OIDC for researcher API access; API key per sensor node
security.transport: TLS for all API and MQTT traffic
security.anonymization: IRB-compliant anonymization pipeline for participant records

constraints.cloud: No cloud services for participant data
constraints.irb: Anonymization within 90 days of session completion

reasoner_reviews.product: The research scope is well-defined: four sensor modalities (CO2, humidity, occupancy infrared, acoustic noise) correlated with cognitive test scores from concurrent study sessions. The data exploration API with Parquet export is the right interface for researcher self-service. The anonymization pipeline timing (within 90 days of session) needs to be operationally automated, not manual.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: FastAPI with async MQTT subscriber is well-suited for concurrent sensor ingestion at campus scale. InfluxDB 2 with bucket-per-building partitioning handles the time-series volume efficiently. The air quality index computation should be a stream processing step applied at ingestion time rather than at query time to keep query latency acceptable. Redis is appropriate for caching rolling 15-minute IAQ window aggregations.
reasoner_reviews.security: Participant data handling requires the most attention. The anonymization pipeline must be automated and auditable, not a manual researcher step. Participant IDs should be pseudonymized at ingestion time with the mapping table stored separately with restricted access. Keycloak OIDC with researcher role gating the study_sessions API is the correct access control model.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: Bare-metal Kubernetes on-premises is non-negotiable for IRB compliance. InfluxDB 2 OSS is free for on-premises deployment. The 8-week timeline to first data collection is aggressive but achievable with the defined MVP scope focusing on CO2 and occupancy sensors first.
reasoner_reviews.critic: The sensor node API key rotation policy is not defined. With potentially hundreds of IoT nodes, key compromise and rotation procedures need to be addressed.

backend.service_design: FastAPI app with four modules: ingestion_api (receives sensor readings via REST from MQTT bridge), query_api (time-range and room-level queries for researchers), anonymization_service (pseudonymizes participant IDs and schedules 90-day anonymization jobs), export_service (generates Parquet files from InfluxDB queries).
backend.api_patterns: REST with OpenAPI 3.0 at /api/v1. Sensor ingestion: POST /ingest/readings (API key auth). Query: GET /data/rooms/{room_id}/air-quality?start=&end=&modality= (OIDC auth). Export: POST /exports/parquet?building=&start=&end= (OIDC researcher role). Anonymization: POST /admin/anonymize-session/{session_id} (OIDC admin role).
backend.background_jobs: Celery worker runs nightly anonymization jobs: scans study_sessions where session_end < NOW() - 90 days and participant_id NOT anonymized, replaces participant_id with a stable pseudonym hash, deletes the mapping table entry. Redis Celery broker.
backend.failure_handling: MQTT ingestion uses QoS 1 to guarantee at-least-once delivery. Duplicate readings deduplicated in InfluxDB by measurement+device_id+timestamp. FastAPI global exception handler returns structured error responses with request_id.

security.auth_design: Keycloak 23 OIDC for researcher and admin roles. Sensor nodes authenticate with per-node API keys stored in Kubernetes Secret. API keys are 256-bit random tokens hashed with SHA-256 before storage.
security.authorization_model: Researcher role: read-only access to aggregated air quality data and Parquet export. Admin role: sensor node management, session management, anonymization trigger. No role can access unanonymized participant_id after the 90-day window.
security.privacy_controls: Participant pseudonymization at ingestion: sensor occupancy events reference internal session_id, not participant name. Participant-to-session mapping table stored in separate restricted PostgreSQL table with admin-only access. Mapping deleted after 90-day anonymization job runs.
security.audit_and_logging_controls: All researcher API queries logged to structured audit log with user_id, query_parameters, timestamp, rows_returned. Anonymization job execution logged with session_ids processed.

data.entities: sensor_nodes (id, node_id TEXT, location_room_id, modalities JSON list, firmware_version, registered_at, api_key_hash). sensor_measurements (InfluxDB measurement: iaq_readings, tags: node_id/room_id/building_id/modality, fields: value FLOAT, unit TEXT, quality_flag INT, timestamp). building_spaces (id, room_id TEXT, building TEXT, floor INT, area_sqm, room_type ENUM classroom/lab/office/corridor). occupancy_events (InfluxDB measurement, tags: room_id, fields: occupant_count INT, session_id TEXT nullable). air_quality_indices (InfluxDB measurement: aqi_computed, tags: room_id, fields: co2_ppm FLOAT, humidity_pct FLOAT, noise_db FLOAT, composite_aqi FLOAT, computed_at). study_sessions (PostgreSQL: id, session_id TEXT, room_id, participant_id TEXT pseudonymized, start_time, end_time, cognitive_score FLOAT, anonymized BOOL). participant_records (PostgreSQL restricted table: participant_id TEXT, real_name ENCRYPTED, session_ids JSON, deleted_at).
data.storage_design: InfluxDB 2 with bucket 'iaq_raw' (30-day retention for raw readings) and bucket 'iaq_aggregated' (5-year retention for hourly aggregations). PostgreSQL 16 for study_sessions and participant_records. Redis 7 for 15-minute rolling window aggregation cache keyed by room_id.
data.retention_and_deletion: Raw sensor bucket: 30-day retention, automatic InfluxDB expiry. Aggregated bucket: 5-year retention. PostgreSQL study_sessions: 5-year retention. Participant mapping: deleted within 90 days of session end by anonymization job.
data.analytics_events: Parquet exports include sensor_measurement, room_id, composite_aqi, cognitive_score (anonymized) for HPC statistical analysis.

devops.deployment_topology: Bare-metal Kubernetes cluster (3 nodes) hosting: FastAPI deployment (2 replicas), Celery worker deployment (1 replica), InfluxDB StatefulSet, PostgreSQL StatefulSet, Redis StatefulSet, EMQX MQTT broker StatefulSet, Grafana + Prometheus deployments.
devops.ci_cd_design: GitLab CI: pytest with 80% coverage gate, mypy type check, flake8 lint on every MR. Merge to main builds Docker image, pushes to university container registry, triggers Helm chart upgrade via kubectl rollout.
devops.observability_stack: Prometheus scrapes FastAPI /metrics endpoint: sensor_ingest_rate_per_sec, query_latency_p99, mqtt_message_lag_seconds, anonymization_jobs_completed. Grafana dashboards: IAQ Ingestion Overview, Query Performance, Anonymization Pipeline Status.
devops.backup_and_recovery: InfluxDB nightly backup to university NAS via influx backup command. PostgreSQL nightly pg_dump to NAS. Kubernetes PV snapshots weekly.

target_output.thinking_summary: First-pass architecture for a campus indoor air quality IoT research prototype. Key decisions: FastAPI async backend for concurrent MQTT ingestion from 100+ sensor nodes, InfluxDB 2 for time-series with dual buckets (raw 30-day and aggregated 5-year), PostgreSQL for study session and participant records, automated Celery anonymization pipeline for GDPR/IRB compliance. The participant data sensitivity and medium risk level require Keycloak OIDC and a robust pseudonymization strategy.
target_output.title: Campus Indoor Air Quality IoT Research Platform
target_output.executive_summary: A bare-metal Kubernetes research prototype for a university campus study correlating indoor air quality measurements with cognitive performance outcomes. IoT sensor nodes across buildings push CO2, humidity, occupancy infrared, and acoustic noise readings via MQTT every 60 seconds. A FastAPI ingestion service writes measurements to InfluxDB 2, computes composite air quality indices, and caches 15-minute rolling window aggregations in Redis. Researchers query a time-range data exploration API and export anonymized Parquet datasets for statistical analysis on university HPC. An automated Celery anonymization pipeline pseudonymizes participant session records within 90 days of study completion per GDPR and IRB requirements.
target_output.architecture_overview: On-premises bare-metal Kubernetes architecture with no cloud services, as mandated by IRB protocol for participant data. Sensor nodes publish MQTT messages to an EMQX broker cluster; a FastAPI MQTT bridge subscribes and writes to InfluxDB 2 buckets partitioned by retention tier (raw 30-day, aggregated 5-year). At ingestion time, a composite air quality index is computed from CO2 ppm, humidity, and acoustic noise values and written to the aqi_computed measurement. Study session records and participant pseudonym mappings are stored in PostgreSQL with role-based access control. Researchers authenticate via Keycloak OIDC and query the FastAPI data exploration API, exporting Parquet files for HPC analysis. A Celery worker processes nightly IRB anonymization jobs, deleting participant-to-session mappings after the 90-day window.
target_output.technology_stack: Backend: Python 3.12, FastAPI 0.110 with asyncio, asyncio-mqtt for MQTT subscription, Pydantic v2 for request validation. Time-series DB: InfluxDB 2 OSS (buckets: iaq_raw 30-day, iaq_aggregated 5-year). Relational DB: PostgreSQL 16 for study sessions and participant records. Cache: Redis 7 for 15-minute rolling IAQ window aggregations. Auth: Keycloak 23 OIDC with python-jose for JWT validation. Async task queue: Celery 5 with Redis broker for anonymization jobs. Export: pyarrow for Parquet file generation. Monitoring: Prometheus Python client, Grafana 10. CI/CD: GitLab CI with Helm chart deployment. MQTT: EMQX 5 cluster for sensor node ingestion.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Sensor Node Registration: admin API to register new nodes with room assignment, modality list, and issued API key. Multi-Modal Ingestion: MQTT subscriber receives CO2 (ppm), relative humidity (%), occupancy count (infrared PIR count), and acoustic noise (dB SPL) from nodes every 60 seconds; validates against node API key and modality list. Composite AQI Computation: ingestion pipeline computes composite_aqi from weighted CO2, humidity deviation from comfort range, and noise level at write time. Data Exploration API: researchers query air quality by room, building, time range, and modality; returns 1-minute aggregations from InfluxDB. Parquet Export: researcher-triggered export of anonymized study session data correlated with IAQ windows as Parquet files for HPC transfer. IRB Anonymization Pipeline: Celery nightly job identifies sessions past 90-day window and replaces real participant IDs with stable pseudonym hashes, deletes mapping table entries.
target_output.system_components: EMQX 5 MQTT broker (2-node cluster, port 1883 with TLS, MQTT topic: iaq/{building_id}/{room_id}/{node_id}/{modality}). FastAPI ingestion service (2 replicas, asyncio-mqtt subscriber + REST ingest endpoint). FastAPI query service (2 replicas, InfluxDB Flux query API client). InfluxDB 2 StatefulSet (2 buckets: iaq_raw, iaq_aggregated). PostgreSQL 16 StatefulSet (study_sessions and participant_records databases). Redis 7 StatefulSet (Celery broker + IAQ rolling window cache). Celery worker (1 replica, anonymization and aggregation jobs). Keycloak 23 StatefulSet for OIDC identity. Grafana + Prometheus for operations monitoring.
target_output.workflows: Sensor Ingestion Workflow: IoT node publishes MQTT message to topic iaq/engineering_building/room_301/node_E01/co2 with payload {value: 842, unit: ppm, timestamp: ISO8601}. EMQX broker validates API key in MQTT username. FastAPI asyncio-mqtt subscriber receives message, validates sensor_node API key hash against PostgreSQL sensor_nodes table, writes raw reading to InfluxDB iaq_raw bucket. Ingestion pipeline queries Redis for current occupancy and noise values for the same room, computes composite_aqi, writes to aqi_computed measurement. Research Query and Export Workflow: Researcher authenticates via Keycloak OIDC, receives JWT with researcher role. Calls GET /api/v1/data/rooms/room_301/air-quality?start=2026-01-01&end=2026-01-31&modality=co2. FastAPI validates JWT, checks researcher role, queries InfluxDB iaq_aggregated bucket via Flux query for 1-minute mean CO2 aggregations. Returns JSON array. For Parquet export, researcher calls POST /api/v1/exports/parquet with building and date range; export_service runs InfluxDB + PostgreSQL join query correlating aqi_computed windows with anonymized study_sessions cognitive scores, generates Parquet file via pyarrow, returns download URL on university NFS.
target_output.data_model: sensor_nodes (id UUID PK, node_id TEXT UNIQUE, room_id TEXT FK, building_id TEXT, modalities TEXT[] e.g. ['co2','humidity','occupancy','acoustic'], firmware_version, api_key_hash TEXT, registered_at TIMESTAMPTZ). building_spaces (room_id TEXT PK, building TEXT, floor INT, area_sqm FLOAT, room_type ENUM 'classroom'/'lab'/'office'/'corridor', capacity INT). study_sessions (id UUID PK, session_id TEXT UNIQUE, room_id TEXT, participant_id TEXT pseudonymized, start_time TIMESTAMPTZ, end_time TIMESTAMPTZ, cognitive_score FLOAT, test_type TEXT e.g. 'n_back_2', anonymized BOOL DEFAULT false, anonymized_at TIMESTAMPTZ). participant_records (restricted table - admin only: participant_uuid UUID PK, session_ids UUID[], created_at, deleted_at). InfluxDB measurements: iaq_raw (tags: node_id, room_id, building_id, modality; fields: value FLOAT, unit TEXT, quality_flag INT). aqi_computed (tags: room_id, building_id; fields: co2_ppm FLOAT, humidity_pct FLOAT, noise_db FLOAT, occupant_count INT, composite_aqi FLOAT).
target_output.api_design: POST /api/v1/ingest/readings - API key auth header X-Node-Key; body {node_id, modality, value, unit, timestamp}. Returns 202 Accepted. GET /api/v1/data/rooms/{room_id}/air-quality - OIDC JWT Bearer; query params: start, end (ISO8601), modality (co2|humidity|occupancy|acoustic|aqi), granularity (1m|15m|1h). Returns array of {timestamp, value, unit}. POST /api/v1/exports/parquet - OIDC researcher role; body {building_id, start, end, include_cognitive_scores: bool}. Returns {export_id, status, download_url nullable}. GET /api/v1/exports/{export_id} - poll export status. POST /api/v1/admin/nodes - OIDC admin role; register new sensor node, returns issued api_key. POST /api/v1/admin/sessions/{session_id}/anonymize - OIDC admin role; trigger immediate anonymization.
target_output.security_and_compliance: GDPR and IRB compliance: participant records stored in PostgreSQL participant_records table with admin-only PostgreSQL role grant. Participant-to-session mapping deleted by Celery anonymization job within 90 days of session_end. Researchers can never query unanonymized participant identities through the data exploration API; participant_id in study_sessions is a stable pseudonym hash after anonymization. All API traffic over TLS 1.3. Keycloak OIDC enforces researcher and admin role separation. Sensor node API keys are 256-bit random tokens; stored as SHA-256 hashes in PostgreSQL. MQTT broker requires TLS client certificate or API key per node. InfluxDB network policy allows connections only from FastAPI pods within the Kubernetes cluster. Audit log of all researcher queries written to structured JSON log with user_id, endpoint, parameters, timestamp.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: Bare-metal Kubernetes (3 nodes: 1 control plane, 2 workers). All services deployed via Helm charts stored in the project GitLab repository. PersistentVolumeClaims for InfluxDB (500 GB), PostgreSQL (50 GB), Redis (10 GB) backed by university SAN storage. Ingress via nginx-ingress with TLS termination. GitLab CI pipeline on merge to main: pytest coverage >= 80%, mypy, flake8, Docker image build and push to university Harbor registry, Helm chart lint, helm upgrade --install to dev namespace. Promotion to production namespace requires manual approval in GitLab pipeline. Nightly backups: influx backup to /mnt/nas/iaq-backups/, pg_dump to /mnt/nas/pg-backups/, retained 30 days. Kubernetes pod disruption budgets ensure at least 1 FastAPI replica available during node maintenance.
target_output.observability: Prometheus scrapes FastAPI /metrics endpoint. Custom metrics: iaq_ingest_messages_total (counter by building and modality), iaq_ingest_latency_seconds (histogram), influxdb_query_duration_seconds (histogram), anonymization_jobs_total (counter by status success/failure), mqtt_broker_message_lag_seconds. Grafana dashboards: IAQ Ingestion Overview (per-building ingest rate, MQTT lag, error rate), Query Performance (p99 latency by endpoint), Anonymization Pipeline (jobs completed, pending sessions past 90-day window). Alerts: ingest rate drops to zero for any building for > 5 minutes (possible MQTT broker or node power issue), p99 query latency > 5 seconds, anonymization job failure.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Zero cloud cost; all components on university bare-metal Kubernetes. InfluxDB 2 OSS is free for on-premises. Expected ingestion volume: 500 nodes x 4 modalities x 1 reading/60s = 2,000 measurements/minute, well within InfluxDB single-node throughput. Raw bucket 30-day retention keeps storage at approximately 50 GB. Aggregated bucket stores hourly means for 5 years (~5 GB). PostgreSQL study_sessions for 10 research cohorts of 200 participants each is under 1 MB.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (8 weeks): MQTT ingestion for CO2 and occupancy modalities, InfluxDB dual-bucket setup, sensor node registration API, basic data exploration API (room-level CO2 time-range query), CSV export, Keycloak OIDC integration, Prometheus + Grafana ingestion dashboard, GitLab CI pipeline, Helm chart deployment. Phase 2 (weeks 9-16): Add humidity and acoustic noise modalities, composite AQI computation pipeline, Parquet export for HPC, Celery anonymization pipeline with 90-day job, PostgreSQL study_sessions model, researcher role-gated cognitive score correlation queries. Phase 3 (post first publication): CO2 predictive alerting via ML anomaly model, acoustic frequency spectrum analysis, federated anonymized data sharing API for partner universities.
target_output.development_guidelines: Python 3.12 with strict type annotations enforced by mypy. flake8 with max line length 120. pytest coverage >= 80% for ingestion, anonymization, and export modules. New sensor modality support added by updating the modality enum in sensor_node config and adding an InfluxDB field mapping in the ingestion config file, without code changes. Alembic migration required for every PostgreSQL schema change. IRB anonymization logic must have 100% test coverage. All API changes require OpenAPI schema update before merge.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: EMQX broker node failure drops sensor readings during the gap. Mitigation: EMQX 2-node cluster with persistent session; sensor nodes configured for QoS 1 reconnect with queued messages. Risk: InfluxDB disk pressure from raw bucket if 30-day retention is insufficient during a long research pause. Mitigation: monitor disk usage via Prometheus alert at 80% PV capacity. Tradeoff: InfluxDB 2 OSS over TimescaleDB chosen for native time-series query language (Flux) and retention policy automation; tradeoff is less familiar SQL query interface for researchers accustomed to PostgreSQL.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Confirmed InfluxDB 2 over TimescaleDB for native retention policy management and Flux query language. Confirmed EMQX over Mosquitto for clustering and MQTT 5.0 support. Confirmed Celery over cron-based anonymization for retry semantics and audit logging. Confirmed Keycloak OIDC over custom JWT issuer to avoid maintaining auth infrastructure in-house.

project.goal: B2B industrial IoT hardware product catalog with global performance optimization

frontend.rendering: Static generation from YAML content files
frontend.search: Pagefind client-side search index over product content

data.content: YAML files for product families and SKUs
data.large_assets: Cloudflare R2 for CAD drawings and PDF spec sheets > 1 MB

devops.ci: GitHub Actions for Astro build and Cloudflare Pages deploy

constraints.seo: All spec pages must be indexable
constraints.cad_size: CAD files up to 50 MB stored in R2

reasoner_reviews.product: The B2B hardware catalog use case is well-defined. Procurement engineers need fast access to spec tables, downloadable PDFs, and firmware changelogs. The round 1 plan lacked specifics on how CAD and spec sheet assets are managed given Cloudflare Pages 25 MB bundle limit for individual files. The Cloudflare R2 decision for large assets was mentioned but not architected.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Astro 4 with zero-JS pages for product content is the right call for a globally fast B2B catalog. The critical gap from round 1 is the large asset delivery architecture - CAD files up to 50 MB must come from R2, not Pages bundle. Product YAML files should drive both the Astro content collection and the search index generation. Pagefind for client-side search is the right zero-server approach.
reasoner_reviews.security: Cloudflare Pages HTTPS and security headers in _headers file are sufficient for a public static site. No user data means minimal security surface. Ensure _headers file disallows X-Frame embedding to prevent clickjacking of the product download links.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: The global 4G < 2s load constraint requires Cloudflare edge serving, image optimization, and critical CSS inlining for above-fold product hero sections. Firmware changelog pages should be statically pre-rendered, not loaded client-side, to ensure crawlability.

frontend.app_structure: Astro 4 content collections: 'products' (YAML files per SKU), 'families' (YAML files per product family), 'changelogs' (Markdown files per firmware version). Dynamic routes: /products/[family]/[sku], /changelogs/[family], /compatibility.
frontend.pages_and_flows: Home page with product family grid and featured SKUs. Product family page with filterable SKU listing. SKU detail page: spec table, key features, download buttons for PDF spec sheet and CAD files (R2 URLs), firmware history link. Firmware changelog page per family: versioned entries with diff highlights and download link. Compatibility matrix page: static table of gateway SKU compatibility with major industrial protocols (Modbus, BACnet, PROFIBUS, OPC-UA).
frontend.performance_notes: Astro generates zero JS for product content pages. Pagefind search index built after Astro build; loaded lazily on search icon click. All product images in WebP/AVIF via Astro image integration. Critical CSS inlined for above-fold product hero. Font subsetting for corporate typeface. Target: LCP < 1.5s on Cloudflare edge globally.
frontend.frontend_risks: Pagefind index size may grow large as SKU count increases beyond 500; mitigate by splitting index by product family.

security.auth_design: No authentication; fully public catalog.
security.abuse_prevention: No user input forms in MVP. Cloudflare Web Application Firewall at edge blocks known attack patterns. R2 presigned URLs for CAD downloads have 1-hour expiry to prevent hotlinking.
security.privacy_controls: Cloudflare Web Analytics is cookieless; no consent banner needed. No third-party tracking scripts. _headers file sets Referrer-Policy: strict-origin-when-cross-origin.

data.entities: product_families (YAML: id, name, description, category, image, protocol_support list). hardware_skus (YAML: id, family_id, name, sku_code, cpu, ram_gb, storage_gb, io_ports list, operating_temp_range, certifications list, spec_sheet_r2_key, cad_drawing_r2_keys list, discontinued BOOL). firmware_releases (Markdown in /src/content/changelogs: version, release_date, product_family, improvements list, fixes list, download_r2_key). compatibility_matrices (YAML: sku_id, protocol, supported BOOL, notes).
data.storage_design: YAML and Markdown content in repository /src/content. Binary assets (PDF spec sheets, CAD files) stored in Cloudflare R2 bucket 'hardware-assets'. R2 presigned download URLs generated at Astro build time for non-expiring public read (or time-limited presigned for access control in phase 2).
data.retention_and_deletion: No user data to retain. Hardware asset files retained indefinitely; superseded firmware versions archived but not deleted for regulatory traceability.

devops.deployment_topology: GitHub repository triggers GitHub Actions on push to main. Astro build generates /dist static output. Pagefind indexes /dist. Cloudflare Pages deployment via Cloudflare Wrangler CLI. R2 asset sync via AWS CLI-compatible Wrangler R2 commands.
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions workflow: TypeScript typecheck, Astro build, Pagefind index build, Wrangler pages deploy. Preview deployment per PR via Cloudflare Pages preview URL. Merge to main deploys to production. Build completes in under 3 minutes.
devops.observability_stack: Cloudflare Web Analytics dashboard for global pageview distribution, top product pages, and TTFB per PoP. Cloudflare Pages build logs for CI monitoring.
devops.devops_risks: Large CAD file additions to repository would bloat git history; mitigate by using R2 for all files > 1 MB and enforcing with a pre-commit hook checking file size.

previous_plan.title: Industrial IoT Hardware Catalog - Round 1
previous_plan.executive_summary: Astro 4 static product catalog on Cloudflare Pages for B2B industrial IoT hardware. Product data driven by YAML content files. Large assets (CAD, PDFs) stored in Cloudflare R2. Pagefind for client-side search.
previous_plan.architecture_overview: Astro builds static product family and SKU pages from YAML content collections. Large binary assets in Cloudflare R2. Cloudflare Web Analytics for cookieless pageview tracking.
