target_output.thinking_summary: This is round 3 of a weak-quality fleet management plan. Two high-severity issues remained unresolved through round 2: the temperature breach alert E2E latency path was unspecified, and the GPS anonymization pg_cron job was never defined. Both are now resolved explicitly: the alert processor traces Kafka consumer to SNS to push notification with breach_alert_e2e_latency_ms instrumented in Datadog at the 60s PagerDuty threshold; and a pg_cron job is defined with schedule, SQL, and failure alerting. The overall plan quality remains weak due to thin phased implementation and guidelines sections.

fix_report.action_taken: Added explicit alert-processor service specification tracing the full path from Kafka consumer offset commit to SNS publish to mobile push notification, with breach_alert_e2e_latency_ms Datadog metric and PagerDuty alert threshold of 60 seconds.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Compliance officers can validate the 60-second SLA is monitored and alerting is in place for the cold-chain regulatory requirement.

fix_report.action_taken: Defined pg_cron job schedule (nightly 02:00 UTC), SQL UPDATE statement anonymizing lat/lng to PostGIS centroid of driver district, and Datadog monitor on pg_cron job failure to PagerDuty.
fix_report.expected_outcome: GDPR 90-day GPS anonymization is automated, auditable, and monitored for failures.

target_output.title: Fleet Telematics Platform - Cold-Chain Edition
target_output.executive_summary: A high-scale real-time fleet management platform for refrigerated logistics operators tracking GPS position, fuel levels, and cargo temperature from up to 500 vehicles every 10 seconds. Built with Go 1.22 ingestion services, Vue 3 MapLibre dashboard, TimescaleDB hypertables, and Kafka on AWS ECS Fargate in eu-west-1. Provides sub-60-second temperature breach alerting with EC 37/2005 audit trail integrity and GDPR-compliant GPS anonymization automation.
target_output.architecture_overview: Event-driven architecture with three Go microservices: telemetry-ingestor consuming from MSK Kafka 'telemetry.raw' topic and writing batched inserts to TimescaleDB gps_positions and fuel_readings hypertables; fleet-api serving Vue 3 SPA via Gin REST and WebSocket hub with Redis pub/sub for cross-instance fan-out to 500+ concurrent dashboard clients; alert-processor consuming 'telemetry.processed' Kafka topic, evaluating cargo_sensor thresholds against active breach rules, and publishing SNS notifications within 60 seconds of threshold crossing. Auth0 RBAC JWT validated at ALB and Gin middleware. Vue 3 SPA with MapLibre GL renders live vehicle positions from Pinia vehicleState store updated via WebSocket frames.
target_output.technology_stack: Frontend: Vue 3, TypeScript, Pinia, MapLibre GL JS, Vite. Backend: Go 1.22, Gin, gorilla/websocket, confluent-kafka-go. Database: TimescaleDB on RDS PostgreSQL 15 with pg_cron and PostGIS extensions. Cache: ElastiCache Redis 7. Queue: MSK Kafka 3-broker, 16 partitions on telemetry.raw topic. Infrastructure: ECS Fargate eu-west-1a/1b/1c, ALB with WAF, MSK, S3 for compliance exports, SNS for push notifications, CloudWatch Logs, Datadog APM.
target_output.functional_feature_map: FleetMap: live MapLibre map with 500 vehicle markers color-coded by cargo temperature status (green/amber/red), updated via WebSocket every 10 seconds. VehicleDetail: cargo sensor gauges per sensor position (front/middle/rear), fuel level bar, current driver assignment, last 4-hour route replay. BreachLog: filterable table of temperature exceedances with duration, vehicle, sensor position, and resolution status; CSV/PDF export for EC 37/2005 compliance reports. GeofenceManager: polygon zone editor with alert-on-exit rules. DriverAssignment: dispatcher interface for assigning drivers to vehicles with shift start/end times. AdminPanel: fleet_manager interface for vehicle registration, sensor calibration records, and alert rule thresholds.
target_output.system_components: MSK Kafka cluster (3 brokers, 16 partitions, telemetry.raw and telemetry.processed topics, 7-day retention). Telemetry-ingestor ECS service (Go, scales 2-8 tasks, consumes telemetry.raw, batch-inserts to TimescaleDB every 500ms, manual offset commit after successful write, DLQ on telemetry.failed topic after 3 retries). Alert-processor ECS service (Go, 2 tasks, consumes telemetry.processed, evaluates threshold rules from Redis rule cache, publishes SNS on breach, records to temperature_breaches table, measures breach_alert_e2e_latency_ms from event timestamp to SNS publish). Fleet-api ECS service (Go Gin, scales 2-4 tasks, REST + WebSocket hub, Redis pub/sub for cross-instance broadcast). TimescaleDB RDS Multi-AZ (chunk_time_interval=1h, 7x compression on gps_positions and fuel_readings, continuous aggregates for hourly summaries). ElastiCache Redis 7 single-primary (vehicle live state HASHes, WebSocket pub/sub channels, alert rule cache). S3 bucket for compliance report PDF/CSV exports. SNS topic for mobile push and email breach notifications.
target_output.workflows: Telemetry Ingestion: IoT unit POSTs GPS+fuel+temperature JSON to ALB /ingest endpoint every 10 seconds; telemetry-ingestor validates payload, publishes to Kafka telemetry.raw; consumer goroutine accumulates 50-record batches and writes to TimescaleDB gps_positions hypertable within 500ms; simultaneously publishes normalized event to telemetry.processed topic. Temperature Breach Detection: alert-processor consumes telemetry.processed; for each cargo_sensor reading, evaluates against active threshold rules loaded from Redis; if recorded_temp exceeds setpoint by >2C for >30 seconds, creates temperature_breaches record, appends SHA-256 hash chain entry, and publishes SNS notification; breach_alert_e2e_latency_ms measured from original event timestamp to SNS publish, target <60 seconds. GPS Anonymization: pg_cron job runs nightly at 02:00 UTC executing 'UPDATE gps_positions SET lat=ST_Y(ST_Centroid(driver_district_polygon)), lng=ST_X(ST_Centroid(driver_district_polygon)) WHERE recorded_at < NOW() - INTERVAL 90 days AND lat IS NOT NULL'; Datadog monitors pg_cron.job_run_details for status != 'succeeded' and pages PagerDuty on failure.
target_output.data_model: vehicles (id UUID PK, fleet_id UUID FK, plate VARCHAR, make, model, reefer_unit_id, active BOOLEAN, created_at). gps_positions (vehicle_id UUID, lat DOUBLE PRECISION, lng DOUBLE PRECISION, speed_kmh SMALLINT, heading SMALLINT, recorded_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL) - TimescaleDB hypertable, partitioned by recorded_at with chunk_time_interval=1h, compressed after 24h. fuel_readings (vehicle_id UUID, level_pct SMALLINT, fuel_litres NUMERIC, recorded_at TIMESTAMPTZ) - hypertable, compressed after 24h. cargo_sensors (id UUID PK, vehicle_id UUID FK, position ENUM front/middle/rear, setpoint_celsius NUMERIC, calibrated_at TIMESTAMPTZ). temperature_breaches (id UUID PK, vehicle_id UUID FK, sensor_id UUID FK, recorded_temp NUMERIC, threshold_celsius NUMERIC, breach_started_at TIMESTAMPTZ, resolved_at TIMESTAMPTZ, prev_hash CHAR(64), row_hash CHAR(64) - append-only with hash chain). driver_assignments (id UUID PK, vehicle_id UUID FK, driver_id UUID FK, start_at TIMESTAMPTZ, end_at TIMESTAMPTZ). geofence_zones (id UUID PK, fleet_id UUID FK, name, polygon PostGIS GEOMETRY, alert_on_exit BOOLEAN).
target_output.api_design: REST /api/v1 with Auth0 JWT Bearer. GET /v1/vehicles?fleet_id= (list with last position, fuel, temp status). GET /v1/vehicles/:id/telemetry?start=&end=&resolution=1h (time-series export). POST /v1/vehicles (fleet_manager only). GET /v1/breaches?vehicle_id=&start=&end=&status=active (compliance officer report query). PATCH /v1/breaches/:id/resolve (dispatcher resolves breach). POST /v1/geofences (fleet_manager creates zone). GET /v1/reports/cold-chain-export?vehicle_id=&period= (generates S3-presigned PDF). WebSocket /ws/v1/fleet-live: client subscribes with fleet_id; server pushes VehicleFrame JSON every 10 seconds with position, temperature_status, fuel_pct. Rate limit: 200 req/min per user.
target_output.security_and_compliance: GDPR controls: lawful basis documented as legitimate interest for fleet operational safety; driver GPS data classified retention_class=short_term with pg_cron anonymization at 90 days; data processing agreements maintained with all logistics operator clients. EC 37/2005: temperature_breaches table uses append-only policy (DELETE privilege revoked) with SHA-256 hash chain per row to detect tampering; 3-year retention enforced by TimescaleDB retention policy. Auth0: RBAC roles fleet_manager/dispatcher/compliance_officer/executive; MFA enforced for fleet_manager and compliance_officer roles; JWT expiry 1 hour. WAF rules block SQLi and XSS at ALB. TLS 1.3 for all external traffic; Redis and MSK use encryption in transit. Secrets in AWS Secrets Manager with 30-day rotation.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: ECS Fargate eu-west-1 across 3 AZs. GitHub Actions pipeline: golangci-lint, go test (75% coverage gate), npm run build (Vue), Docker multi-stage build, ECR push, ECS rolling deploy with 50% minimum healthy. Environments: dev (single AZ, shared MSK), staging (multi-AZ, MSK clone, RDS snapshot restore weekly), production (multi-AZ, MSK 3-broker, RDS Multi-AZ with daily automated snapshots). Auto-scaling: telemetry-ingestor 2-8 tasks on Kafka consumer lag >200; fleet-api 2-4 tasks on CPU 60%. pg_cron extension enabled on RDS with job monitoring. Secrets Manager for Auth0 client secrets and DB credentials with automatic rotation. Rollback: ECS rollback to previous task definition on >3% 5xx in first 10 minutes.
target_output.observability: Datadog APM sidecar on all Fargate tasks. Custom metrics: kafka_consumer_lag.telemetry_raw (PagerDuty at >500), breach_alert_e2e_latency_ms histogram (PagerDuty at p95>60000ms), websocket_connected_clients gauge, telemetry_ingest_rate_per_second, temperature_breach_active_count. CloudWatch Logs with structured JSON and correlation IDs. pg_cron.job_run_details monitored via Datadog log-based metric: alert on job status != 'succeeded' for the GPS anonymization job.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: ~$3,400/month at 500 vehicles: MSK 3-broker ~$600, RDS TimescaleDB Multi-AZ ~$900, ECS Fargate 14 tasks average ~$700, ElastiCache Redis ~$150, ALB+WAF ~$200, S3+SNS ~$50, Datadog ~$800. TimescaleDB compression reduces gps_positions storage 7x vs plain PostgreSQL. Auto-scaling caps ingestor at 8 tasks to stay under $4,000 budget ceiling.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (8 weeks): telemetry-ingestor with Kafka pipeline, fleet-api with live map WebSocket, temperature breach alerting with SNS, Auth0 RBAC, GDPR anonymization pg_cron job, compliance report export for 50-vehicle pilot. Phase 2 (6 weeks): geofence zone management, driver assignment tracking, route history replay, fuel trend analytics, multi-fleet admin panel. Phase 3 (4 weeks): continuous aggregates for daily/weekly fleet summaries, performance dashboards for compliance officers, anomaly detection on fuel drain rates.
target_output.development_guidelines: Go: golangci-lint with errcheck and staticcheck enabled. 75% coverage for ingestion and alert logic. Vue 3: ESLint + Prettier, Vitest for Pinia store unit tests. Trunk-based development with feature flags for dashboard sections. ADR required for any change to TimescaleDB chunk interval or Kafka partition count. DB migrations via golang-migrate with forward-only policy.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: TimescaleDB continuous aggregate refresh lag during high-write periods causing stale dashboard summaries. Mitigation: schedule refreshes during low-traffic windows (03:00-05:00 UTC) and show 'last updated' timestamps on analytics widgets. Risk: MSK broker failure causing telemetry gap. Mitigation: IoT units buffer last 100 records locally and replay on reconnect; Kafka 3-broker cluster tolerates single-broker failure. Tradeoff: Redis single-primary for pub/sub over Redis Cluster avoids slot-based routing complexity; acceptable given pub/sub is non-persistent. Risk: Auth0 outage blocking fleet operations. Mitigation: short-lived JWT cache allows 1-hour operation without re-authentication.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Selected TimescaleDB over InfluxDB for native PostGIS support enabling geofence zone queries. Confirmed pg_cron over Lambda for GPS anonymization to keep automation within the RDS instance. Chose MapLibre GL over Google Maps to eliminate per-tile billing. Kafka manual offset commits confirmed over auto-commit to prevent telemetry data loss on ingestor crash.

metadata.notes: Round 3 fleet management: resolved breach alert latency and GPS anonymization issues; plan quality remains weak with thin phased implementation and guidelines.

project_goal.value: Open-source C++ SDK for zero-copy binary MQTT payload parsing and encoding for embedded IoT devices, with schema versioning and optional Boost.Asio async I/O bindings
project_goal.rationale: Embedded developers need a high-performance codec library that avoids heap allocation in hot paths

target_users.value: Embedded C++ developers building firmware for IoT sensor devices and gateways who need deterministic-performance binary protocol codecs
target_users.rationale: Primary audience identified from GitHub issues on existing tools

project_class.rationale: Distributed as a compiled library and header package, not a hosted service

capabilities.rationale: Pure codec library with no frontend or hosted service components

complexity_level.rationale: Zero-copy deserialization, compile-time field validation, and async Boost.Asio bindings require deep C++ template metaprogramming

risk_level.rationale: Library with no hosted infrastructure; security surface limited to the API contract

data_sensitivity.rationale: Library processes caller-owned data; no data stored or transmitted by the SDK itself

external_exposure.rationale: Open-source published on GitHub and distributed via vcpkg registry

access_model.value: Public open-source under MIT license; no authentication required for library use
access_model.rationale: Open-source model to maximize adoption in embedded developer community

feature_scope.value: Schema YAML definition, codegen for C++ structs and encode/decode functions, zero-copy deserialization via span-based buffer views, schema versioning with migration helpers, Boost.Asio async read/write adapters
feature_scope.rationale: Full feature set for production-grade embedded protocol SDK

mvp_scope.value: Schema YAML parser, C++ struct codegen, encode/decode with zero-copy reads, unit test suite with AddressSanitizer checks
mvp_scope.rationale: Core codec functionality needed before async bindings

security_baseline.value: All decode functions validate payload length before field access; AddressSanitizer and UBSan in CI; clang-tidy with bugprone checks
security_baseline.rationale: Memory safety is the primary security concern for a C++ library used in firmware

frontend_stack.value: none
frontend_stack.rationale: Library SDK with no frontend

backend_stack.value: C++17 with CMake 3.26 build system; optional Boost.Asio 1.84 for async bindings
backend_stack.rationale: C++17 provides std::string_view and if constexpr for zero-copy and compile-time validation

data_platform.value: none - library processes in-memory buffers with no persistent storage
data_platform.rationale: Codec library with no database dependency

hosting_target.value: GitHub Releases for source archives and vcpkg registry for package distribution; Codecov for coverage reporting
hosting_target.rationale: Standard C++ open-source distribution channels

privacy_retention_policy.value: No user data stored; library collects no telemetry or crash reports
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: Open-source library with no telemetry infrastructure

future_scope.value: Python and Rust bindings via FFI, MQTT v5 property encoding support, FlatBuffers schema compatibility layer
future_scope.rationale: Ecosystem expansion after stable v1.0 release

constraints.value: Must compile on GCC 9+, Clang 12+, MSVC 19.29+; decode functions must complete in O(1) with no heap allocation in hot path; compiled size must not exceed 500 KB with -Os
constraints.rationale: Firmware environments have strict binary size and allocator constraints

observability_baseline.value: Codecov coverage reporting per PR; CI benchmark suite measuring parse throughput and peak heap usage across GCC, Clang, MSVC; benchmark results published to GitHub Pages
observability_baseline.rationale: Performance regression detection is critical for a high-performance codec library

execution_preference.value: Feature branches with mandatory PR review from 2 core maintainers; semantic versioning with CHANGELOG
execution_preference.rationale: Open-source governance model with controlled release cadence

project.goal: Zero-copy C++ MQTT binary codec SDK with schema versioning
project.timeline: v1.0 in 12 weeks, Boost.Asio bindings in v1.1

backend.build_system: CMake 3.26 with FetchContent for optional Boost.Asio
backend.api: Header-only core, optional compiled async bindings

security.memory_safety: AddressSanitizer and UBSan in CI on all test targets
security.static_analysis: clang-tidy with modernize and bugprone checks enabled as errors

data.format: custom binary framing defined by schema YAML with magic bytes, version, length, CRC16

devops.distribution: vcpkg port manifest and GitHub Releases

reasoner_reviews.product: Zero-copy deserialization is the core performance differentiator for firmware developers. Schema versioning with migration helpers prevents silent data corruption when a device receives a new firmware build with updated message definitions over OTA. The codegen approach is correct since runtime reflection would be too costly for constrained targets.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: The std::span-based zero-copy decode design requires a polyfill span header to maintain C++17 compatibility since std::span is C++20. Codec configurations should be constexpr to allow compile-time schema validation. The Boost.Asio adapter must follow the CompletionToken model so callers can use coroutines, callbacks, or futures without SDK modifications.
reasoner_reviews.security: Buffer overflow prevention is the primary concern. Every decode function must check payload.size() >= expected_frame_size before any field access. AddressSanitizer must run on all test suites in CI, not only sanitizer-specific targets. UBSan must catch signed integer overflow in field offset arithmetic and length calculations.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: vcpkg port manifest must specify exact CMake targets so consumers can use find_package without knowing internal target names. The benchmark regression threshold of 5% is appropriate but the CI benchmark must pin to a specific runner type to avoid false failures from GitHub runner hardware variance.

backend.service_design: Header-only core library in include/mqtt_codec/ with Encoder, Decoder, PayloadFrame, and FieldDescriptor templates. Optional compiled library target mqtt_codec_asio for Boost.Asio adapters. Codegen tool mqtt_codegen is a standalone C++ executable reading schema YAML and outputting header files.
backend.api_patterns: Public API: mqtt_codec::decode<MyMsg>(std::span<const uint8_t>) returning std::expected<MyMsg, DecodeError>; mqtt_codec::encode(const MyMsg& msg, std::span<uint8_t> out_buf) returning size_t bytes written. Schema versioning via SchemaVersion<Major, Minor> template parameter.
backend.failure_handling: Decode errors returned as std::expected variants: BufferTooShort, UnknownVersion, FieldChecksumMismatch, MagicMismatch. No exceptions thrown to support firmware builds with -fno-exceptions.
backend.backend_risks: ABI breakage risk on minor version bumps if callers link against compiled async bindings. Mitigation: header-only core avoids ABI issues; async bindings versioned separately with stable C ABI wrapper.

data.entities: message_schemas (YAML: name, fields array with type/offset/presence, schema_version, crc16 flag). payload_frames (binary: 2-byte magic, 1-byte version_major, 1-byte version_minor, 2-byte payload_length LE, N-byte fields, 2-byte CRC16 optional). field_descriptors (compile-time constexpr: byte_offset, type_tag, has_default).
data.schema_notes: Schema YAML parsed by codegen tool into a C++ header with a static constexpr SchemaDescriptor. Migration helpers are optional lambda arguments to the decode overload for converting v1 messages to v2 structs at decode time without allocating.
data.data_risks: Schema version mismatch between firmware and server may silently truncate optional fields if version check is skipped. Mitigation: strict version matching mode flag in CodecConfiguration with compile-time assertion option.

devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions matrix: os=[ubuntu-24.04, macos-14, windows-2022] x compiler=[gcc-13, clang-18, msvc-2022]. Jobs: build-and-test with ASAN+UBSan on Linux/macOS, benchmark on pinned ubuntu-24.04 self-hosted runner comparing vs baseline branch artifact, coverage with gcov and Codecov upload gated at 85%, static-analysis with clang-tidy.
devops.observability_stack: Codecov dashboard for per-file coverage trends with branch coverage. Benchmark results stored as JSON artifacts and published to GitHub Pages. Badge in README for coverage percentage and benchmark status.
devops.devops_risks: GitHub shared runner hardware variance causes false benchmark failures. Mitigation: benchmark job uses self-hosted runner with pinned hardware spec or widens regression threshold to 10% on shared runners.

target_output.thinking_summary: Designing a zero-copy C++17 MQTT payload codec library. Key decisions: header-only core using tcbrindle/span polyfill for C++17 compatibility, decode functions returning std::expected to avoid exceptions in -fno-exceptions firmware builds, codegen tool outputting constexpr SchemaDescriptors for compile-time field validation, and benchmark CI on a pinned self-hosted runner to prevent false regression alerts from GitHub runner variance. Architecture is entirely about the library API surface, build system, and distribution pipeline.
target_output.title: mqtt-codec: Zero-Copy C++17 MQTT Payload SDK
target_output.executive_summary: An open-source C++17 header-only library for zero-copy binary MQTT payload encoding and decoding targeting embedded IoT firmware. Developers define message schemas in YAML; the mqtt_codegen tool emits type-safe C++ structs and encode/decode functions. Decode operations use std::span buffer views with no heap allocation in the hot path. Schema versioning with migration helpers prevents silent data corruption on OTA firmware updates. Optional Boost.Asio async adapters follow the CompletionToken model for coroutine and callback compatibility.
target_output.architecture_overview: Single-repository library with three components: header-only core in include/mqtt_codec/ providing Encoder, Decoder, PayloadFrame, and FieldDescriptor templates using C++17 if constexpr and std::string_view; standalone codegen executable mqtt_codegen that reads schema YAML via yaml-cpp and emits C++ headers with constexpr SchemaDescriptors; optional compiled target mqtt_codec_asio wrapping core decode/encode in Boost.Asio async_read and async_write CompletionToken handlers. CMake 3.26 with FetchContent for optional Boost.Asio. vcpkg port manifest enables one-command consumer integration. CI matrix validates GCC 13, Clang 18, and MSVC 2022 across Linux, macOS, and Windows.
target_output.technology_stack: Language: C++17 with tcbrindle/span polyfill for std::span backcompat. Build: CMake 3.26 with CTest and CPack. Error handling: tl::expected polyfill bundled under include/mqtt_codec/expected.hpp. Codegen: standalone C++ executable using yaml-cpp for YAML parsing. Optional dep: Boost.Asio 1.84 via CMake FetchContent, disabled by default. Distribution: vcpkg port plus GitHub Releases. CI: GitHub Actions with AddressSanitizer, UBSan, clang-tidy, gcov, Codecov. Benchmarks: Google Benchmark framework with JSON artifact output.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Schema Definition: YAML file specifying message name, fields with type/offset/presence_bit/default, schema_version major.minor, and optional CRC16 flag. Codegen: mqtt_codegen reads YAML and outputs MyMsg.hpp with struct definition and static constexpr SchemaDescriptor. Encode: mqtt_codec::encode writes magic, version, length, field bytes in schema order, optional CRC16, returns bytes_written. Decode: mqtt_codec::decode<MyMsg>(span) validates framing, reads fields with zero copy for string_view fields pointing into caller span, returns std::expected<MyMsg, DecodeError>. Schema Migration: decode<MyMsg, FromV1>(span, migration_lambda) applies caller-provided conversion lambda at decode time. Async Bindings: AsyncDecoder<MyMsg> wraps a Boost.Asio AsyncReadStream and returns via CompletionToken.
target_output.system_components: include/mqtt_codec/encoder.hpp: Encoder<SchemaT> template with constexpr field layout. include/mqtt_codec/decoder.hpp: Decoder<SchemaT> with span-based zero-copy field access and length validation. include/mqtt_codec/payload_frame.hpp: PayloadFrame with magic, version, length, and CRC16 accessors over raw span. include/mqtt_codec/schema_version.hpp: SchemaVersion<Major,Minor> template with compatibility check. include/mqtt_codec/errors.hpp: DecodeError enum with BufferTooShort, UnknownVersion, FieldChecksumMismatch, MagicMismatch variants. tools/mqtt_codegen/: YAML-to-C++ code generator executable. src/asio/async_decoder.hpp: AsyncDecoder template compiled with Boost.Asio. tests/: GoogleTest suite with ASAN/UBSan targets. benchmarks/: Google Benchmark suite with 1M synthetic payload corpus.
target_output.workflows: Schema Authoring to Codegen: developer writes sensors/temperature_msg.yaml defining fields sensor_id u16, temperature_raw i16, flags u8, schema_version 1.1; runs cmake --build --target codegen; generated TemperatureMsg.hpp appears in build/generated/; developer includes the header and calls mqtt_codec::decode<TemperatureMsg>(buffer_span) returning std::expected. CI Benchmark Regression Check: PR triggers GitHub Actions benchmark job on pinned ubuntu-24.04 self-hosted runner; job builds benchmarks, runs against 1M synthetic payload corpus, compares throughput and peak heap JSON against benchmark-baseline branch artifact; regression beyond 5% fails the job and posts a comparison table as a PR comment.
target_output.data_model: message_schemas (YAML): name STRING, schema_version STRING semver, fields ARRAY of name/type enum(u8/u16/u32/i8/i16/i32/f32/bytes/string_view)/byte_offset/presence_bit optional/default optional, crc16 BOOL, magic UINT16. payload_frames (binary wire): bytes 0-1 magic u16, byte 2 version_major u8, byte 3 version_minor u8, bytes 4-5 payload_length u16 LE, bytes 6 to N field bytes, bytes N+1 to N+2 CRC16 optional. field_descriptors (compile-time): constexpr FieldDescriptor<T> with offset, presence_bit_index, has_default. schema_versions: SchemaVersion<Major,Minor> template with static compatibility matrix for migration path validation.
target_output.api_design: C++ template API with no HTTP endpoints. mqtt_codec::decode<MyMsg>(std::span<const uint8_t>) -> std::expected<MyMsg, DecodeError>. mqtt_codec::decode<MyMsg, FromVer>(span, MigrationFn&&) -> std::expected<MyMsg, DecodeError>. mqtt_codec::encode(const MyMsg&, std::span<uint8_t>) -> std::expected<size_t, EncodeError>. mqtt_codec::frame_size<MyMsg>() -> constexpr size_t. mqtt_codec::asio::async_decode<MyMsg>(stream, token) -> async completion op. CMake consumer interface: find_package(mqtt_codec REQUIRED) then target_link_libraries(my_target mqtt_codec::core).
target_output.security_and_compliance: Memory safety: every decode path checks payload.size() >= PayloadFrame::min_frame_size() and payload.size() >= header.payload_length + frame_overhead before any field access, making buffer overread impossible. AddressSanitizer and UBSan enabled on Linux and macOS CI jobs for all test targets. clang-tidy checks bugprone-*, modernize-*, cert-* rules with -warnings-as-errors. No heap allocation in decode hot path: string_view fields point into caller-owned span. CRC16 optional validation prevents corrupted payloads from silently producing wrong field values. MIT license; no telemetry collected by the SDK.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: Distribution: cmake --install packages headers to CMAKE_INSTALL_PREFIX and generates mqtt_codecConfig.cmake for find_package. vcpkg port manifest in vcpkg-ports/mqtt-codec/ with portfile.cmake and vcpkg.json specifying version and feature flags. GitHub Actions CI matrix: 6 configurations across 3 OS and 2 sanitizer modes. Release process: maintainer tags vMAJOR.MINOR.PATCH triggering Actions workflow that runs full matrix, packages source archive, uploads to GitHub Releases, and opens vcpkg registry PR. Documentation: Doxygen generated from annotated headers and deployed to GitHub Pages on release tag. Benchmark baseline artifact updated on main branch merge when throughput improves.
target_output.observability: Codecov dashboard with per-file line and branch coverage; gate at 85% blocks PR merge. Google Benchmark JSON artifacts uploaded per CI run; regression detection step compares vs baseline branch artifact and posts diff table to PR. Doxygen API coverage tracked via doxygen-coverage script in CI. reviewdog posts clang-tidy findings as inline PR review comments.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Zero hosting cost: GitHub Actions free tier covers CI for the open-source repository. vcpkg registry submission is free. Self-hosted benchmark runner requires a consistent hardware node; a $5/month VPS with pinned CPU is sufficient. Google Benchmark handles up to 100M iterations per benchmark function providing statistically stable throughput measurements.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 v1.0 (8 weeks): CMake project scaffold with span polyfill, PayloadFrame framing, Encoder and Decoder templates for fixed-width integer and bytes fields, mqtt_codegen YAML parser and C++ header emitter, GoogleTest suite with ASAN and UBSan, Codecov integration, vcpkg port manifest, GitHub Release workflow. Phase 2 v1.1 (4 weeks): string_view zero-copy fields, SchemaVersion template with compatibility check, migration lambda decode overload, Google Benchmark suite with CI regression gate on self-hosted runner. Phase 3 v1.2 (4 weeks): Boost.Asio AsyncDecoder and AsyncEncoder CompletionToken adapters, Doxygen documentation site, CRC16 configurable checksum support.
target_output.development_guidelines: C++17 strict mode; no C++20 features without a backcompat polyfill. clang-format with LLVM style enforced in CI. Every decode function must have a corresponding fuzz target in tests/fuzz/ using libFuzzer. PRs require 2 maintainer approvals. CHANGELOG updated on every PR. Semantic versioning: minor version bump for new field types, major for binary wire format changes. No external dependencies in core headers; yaml-cpp is codegen-only.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: C++17 lacks std::expected; mitigation: bundle tl::expected polyfill under include/mqtt_codec/expected.hpp with a compatibility shim. Risk: GitHub shared runner hardware variance causes false benchmark failures; mitigation: self-hosted runner for benchmarks or widen threshold to 10%. Tradeoff: header-only core versus compiled library avoids ABI issues at cost of compile time for large consumer projects; async bindings remain compiled to contain Boost.Asio dependency. Risk: vcpkg registry PR review latency delaying releases; mitigation: document manual overlay port as a fallback installation path.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Selected tl::expected over exceptions for decode errors to support firmware builds with -fno-exceptions. Confirmed C++17 as minimum standard over C++20 to maximize compiler support matrix including embedded toolchains. Google Benchmark chosen over nanobench for its JSON output format compatible with the CI regression comparison script. MIT license selected over Apache 2.0 to eliminate patent clause friction for firmware adoption.

metadata.notes: First-pass strong-quality C++17 SDK for zero-copy MQTT binary codec targeting embedded firmware developers.

project_goal.value: TypeScript/JavaScript SDK providing a unified abstraction over multiple home energy monitor vendor APIs (Shelly, Emporia, Sense) with built-in data normalization, OAuth token refresh, and rolling aggregation helpers
project_goal.rationale: Application developers need a single interface instead of integrating three incompatible vendor APIs

target_users.value: Node.js and Bun application developers building home energy dashboards, automations, and analytics tools who want to support multiple monitor brands without vendor-specific code
target_users.rationale: Primary audience from energy monitoring developer community

project_class.rationale: Published as an npm package; no hosted service

capabilities.rationale: SDK integrates vendor APIs, normalizes data, and provides aggregation utilities

complexity_level.rationale: Three vendor adapters with OAuth flows and streaming aggregation require careful abstraction but no distributed systems

risk_level.rationale: Library distributing no hosted infrastructure; primary risk is vendor API change breaking SDK consumers

data_sensitivity.rationale: Home energy consumption data is personal data under GDPR when associated with a residential address

external_exposure.rationale: Published on npm registry; publicly accessible documentation

access_model.value: Public npm package under MIT license; no authentication required for library use; callers supply their own vendor credentials
access_model.rationale: Open-source model; credential management is caller responsibility

feature_scope.value: Vendor adapters for Shelly, Emporia, Sense; OAuth2 token refresh automation; response normalization to EnergyReading; rolling aggregation API with minute/hour/day granularity; rate limiting per vendor constraints; TypeScript type exports
feature_scope.rationale: Full SDK feature set required for production energy monitoring applications

mvp_scope.value: Shelly and Emporia adapters, EnergyReading normalization, 1-hour aggregation, full TypeScript types, 90% test coverage
mvp_scope.rationale: Two vendor adapters cover 80% of target user installs; Sense adapter in v1.1

security_baseline.value: Vendor credentials never logged; OAuth tokens stored in caller-provided credential store interface; HTTPS enforced for all vendor API calls; no credential persistence in SDK
security_baseline.rationale: SDK handling OAuth tokens for home energy accounts must protect credentials

frontend_stack.value: none
frontend_stack.rationale: Library SDK with no frontend

backend_stack.value: TypeScript with Bun runtime for tests and build; targets Node.js 18+ and Bun 1.x as consumer runtimes
backend_stack.rationale: TypeScript ensures type-safe API surface; Bun for fast test execution; targets both major JS runtimes

data_platform.value: none - SDK processes in-memory streams; callers manage persistence
data_platform.rationale: SDK is stateless except for in-memory token cache passed via caller credential store

hosting_target.value: npm registry under @energy-sdk/core package name; GitHub Packages as secondary registry
hosting_target.rationale: Standard npm distribution for Node.js and Bun ecosystem

privacy_retention_policy.value: SDK stores no data; security guide documents GDPR obligations for SDK consumers processing EU residential energy data
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: GDPR compliance is caller responsibility; SDK must document this in its security guide

future_scope.value: Sense vendor adapter, WebSocket real-time streaming mode, React hooks package @energy-sdk/react, CLI tool for ad-hoc queries
future_scope.rationale: Post-v1.0 ecosystem expansion

constraints.value: Zero runtime dependencies in core package; adapter packages may depend on vendor-specific OAuth libraries; tree-shakeable ESM exports; bundle size under 15 KB gzipped for core
constraints.rationale: Consumer applications need minimal bundle impact

observability_baseline.value: Codecov with 90% coverage gate; GitHub Actions CI on Node 18/20 and Bun 1.x; integration test suite against vendor API sandbox environments
observability_baseline.rationale: High coverage required given SDK handles credential flows and data normalization

execution_preference.value: Monorepo with Bun workspaces; semantic versioning; changeset-based releases
execution_preference.rationale: Monorepo enables shipping core and adapter packages together

compliance_context.value: GDPR security guide required as part of SDK documentation for consumers processing EU residential energy consumption data associated with household addresses

project.goal: Unified SDK abstracting Shelly, Emporia, and Sense energy monitor APIs
project.timeline: v1.0 in 8 weeks with Shelly and Emporia adapters

backend.build: Bun build for ESM output, tsc for type declarations
backend.api: Class-based vendor adapters implementing EnergyMonitorAdapter interface

security.credential_handling: Credentials passed via CredentialStore interface; never logged or persisted by SDK
security.transport: HTTPS enforced; TLS certificate validation always enabled

data.format: EnergyReading normalized type; AggregationResult for windowed stats
data.vendor_apis: Shelly REST, Emporia GraphQL, Sense WebSocket

devops.ci: GitHub Actions on Node 18, Node 20, Bun 1.x

reasoner_reviews.product: The unified EnergyMonitorAdapter interface is the core value proposition. Shelly uses REST, Emporia uses GraphQL, and Sense uses WebSocket, so the adapter abstraction must handle fundamentally different transport models. Rolling aggregation is a high-value feature since most callers need hourly kWh totals, not raw circuit-level samples.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: The CredentialStore interface pattern is correct for OAuth token lifecycle without storing secrets in the SDK. The async generator pattern for streaming readings from Sense WebSocket will compose well with the aggregation API if aggregation accepts AsyncIterable<EnergyReading>. ESM-only output is safe for Node 18+ and Bun; avoid dual CJS/ESM builds to keep bundle complexity low.
reasoner_reviews.security: Vendor OAuth tokens are the primary secret. The CredentialStore interface must require read and write methods so the SDK can refresh tokens without the caller re-authenticating. Credentials must never appear in thrown Error messages or debug logs. The GDPR security guide must document that EnergyReading objects contain personal data and advise callers on storage and retention.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: Zero runtime dependencies in core is achievable since normalization and aggregation are pure functions. Adapter packages can each depend on vendor-specific SDKs if needed. Tree-shaking requires named ESM exports with no side effects at module level. 15 KB gzipped target is achievable if adapters are separate packages.

backend.service_design: Monorepo with packages: @energy-sdk/core (EnergyMonitorAdapter interface, EnergyReading type, AggregationEngine, CredentialStore interface), @energy-sdk/shelly (ShellyAdapter), @energy-sdk/emporia (EmporiaAdapter), @energy-sdk/sense (SenseAdapter planned v1.1).
backend.api_patterns: EnergyMonitorAdapter interface: getCircuits() -> Promise<Circuit[]>, getReadings(circuitId, options) -> AsyncIterable<EnergyReading>, getAggregated(circuitId, window) -> Promise<AggregationResult>. AggregationEngine.aggregate(source: AsyncIterable<EnergyReading>, granularity) -> AggregationResult.
backend.failure_handling: VendorApiError wraps HTTP status and vendor error codes. TokenExpiredError triggers automatic refresh via CredentialStore. RateLimitError includes retry_after_seconds from vendor response header. All errors extend EnergySDKError base class.
backend.backend_risks: Vendor API breaking changes may silently change response shape. Mitigation: runtime schema validation with zod in each adapter to catch unexpected vendor response structures before normalization.

data.entities: EnergyReading (circuitId STRING, vendorId STRING, timestamp Date, kw_import FLOAT, kw_export FLOAT optional, voltage FLOAT optional, frequency FLOAT optional, source VendorEnum). Circuit (id STRING, name STRING, vendorId STRING, panel STRING optional, phases INT). AggregationResult (circuitId, windowStart Date, windowEnd Date, granularity GranularityEnum, total_kwh FLOAT, min_kw FLOAT, max_kw FLOAT, avg_kw FLOAT, samples INT). VendorCredentials (vendorId, accessToken STRING, refreshToken STRING optional, expiresAt Date optional).
data.schema_notes: Shelly provides per-circuit active power in watts at 1-minute intervals via REST polling. Emporia provides multi-circuit readings via GraphQL query with 1-minute minimum granularity. Normalization converts all readings to kW (divide watts by 1000) and UTC timestamps.
data.data_risks: Emporia GraphQL schema changes without versioning. Mitigation: zod schema validation on GraphQL response; adapter CI integration tests run against Emporia sandbox on every PR.

devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: bun install, bun test (all packages), bun run build (tsc + bun build ESM), Codecov upload. Matrix: [node-18, node-20, bun-1.x]. Changeset bot checks for changeset file on every PR. Release job: changeset version, npm publish with --provenance flag, GitHub Release tag.
devops.observability_stack: Codecov with 90% gate and branch coverage. Bundle size tracked via bundlemon action comparing PR vs main. npm package size reported in CI summary.
devops.devops_risks: Vendor sandbox environments may have rate limits causing flaky integration tests. Mitigation: mock vendor HTTP responses in unit tests using msw; integration tests tagged and run only on schedule or manually.

target_output.thinking_summary: Designing a TypeScript SDK that abstracts three incompatible home energy monitor vendor APIs. Key decisions: EnergyMonitorAdapter interface as the central abstraction, CredentialStore interface pattern for OAuth token lifecycle without secrets in the SDK, async generator return type for streaming readings so aggregation composes via AsyncIterable, monorepo with separate adapter packages to keep core bundle under 15 KB, and zod runtime schema validation in each adapter to catch vendor API changes before normalization.
target_output.title: @energy-sdk: Unified Home Energy Monitor SDK
target_output.executive_summary: A TypeScript monorepo SDK providing a unified EnergyMonitorAdapter interface over Shelly, Emporia, and Sense home energy monitor APIs. Core package handles data normalization to a common EnergyReading type and rolling aggregation with minute, hour, and day granularity. OAuth token refresh and vendor rate limiting are managed transparently. Adapter packages are independently publishable to minimize consumer bundle impact. Targets Node.js 18+ and Bun 1.x with zero runtime dependencies in the core package.
target_output.architecture_overview: Monorepo using Bun workspaces with four packages: @energy-sdk/core defines the EnergyMonitorAdapter interface, EnergyReading and AggregationResult types, AggregationEngine, and CredentialStore interface; @energy-sdk/shelly implements ShellyAdapter using Shelly REST polling API; @energy-sdk/emporia implements EmporiaAdapter over Emporia GraphQL with zod response validation; @energy-sdk/sense implements SenseAdapter via Sense WebSocket feed (planned v1.1). All adapters emit AsyncIterable<EnergyReading> consumed by AggregationEngine. OAuth token refresh is handled inside each adapter using the caller-provided CredentialStore implementation.
target_output.technology_stack: Language: TypeScript 5.4 with strict mode. Runtime targets: Node.js 18+ and Bun 1.x. Build: bun build for ESM output; tsc for .d.ts declarations. Validation: zod 3 in adapter packages for vendor response schema validation. Testing: bun test with @testing-library/msw for vendor HTTP mocking. Distribution: npm with provenance attestation and GitHub Packages. CI: GitHub Actions. Coverage: Codecov. Bundle monitoring: bundlemon.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Vendor Abstraction: EnergyMonitorAdapter interface with getCircuits(), getReadings(circuitId, options) returning AsyncIterable<EnergyReading>, and getAggregated(circuitId, window). Credential Management: CredentialStore interface with async read(vendorId) and write(vendorId, credentials) methods; adapters call write after successful OAuth refresh so callers persist updated tokens. Data Normalization: ShellyAdapter converts per-circuit watt readings and Unix timestamps to EnergyReading kW and UTC Date; EmporiaAdapter normalizes GraphQL energy_usage_kwh values. Rolling Aggregation: AggregationEngine.aggregate(source, granularity) consumes AsyncIterable<EnergyReading> in streaming fashion computing total_kwh, min_kw, max_kw, avg_kw per window. Rate Limiting: each adapter enforces vendor-specific rate limits (Shelly: 1 req/sec per device, Emporia: 60 req/min) using a token bucket per adapter instance.
target_output.system_components: packages/core/src/adapter.ts: EnergyMonitorAdapter interface definition. packages/core/src/types.ts: EnergyReading, Circuit, AggregationResult, VendorCredentials, GranularityEnum TypeScript types. packages/core/src/aggregation.ts: AggregationEngine class with aggregate(AsyncIterable<EnergyReading>, granularity) method. packages/core/src/credential-store.ts: CredentialStore interface with read and write methods. packages/shelly/src/shelly-adapter.ts: ShellyAdapter implementing REST polling with 1-minute interval, token-bucket rate limiter, zod response schema. packages/emporia/src/emporia-adapter.ts: EmporiaAdapter over GraphQL with zod schema validation and OAuth2 refresh flow. packages/core/src/errors.ts: EnergySDKError, VendorApiError, TokenExpiredError, RateLimitError hierarchy.
target_output.workflows: Application Initialization: caller instantiates ShellyAdapter with a CredentialStore implementation and device IP list; calls getCircuits() to enumerate circuits; SDK performs initial token validation and caches circuit metadata. Continuous Reading Aggregation: caller calls getAggregated(circuitId, {granularity: 'hour', start, end}) which internally creates an AsyncIterable<EnergyReading> by polling Shelly REST at 1-minute intervals, feeds it through AggregationEngine, and returns AggregationResult with total_kwh and stats for each hour bucket. Token Refresh Flow: EmporiaAdapter calls vendorClient.getEnergyUsage(); on 401 response, adapter calls credentialStore.read(vendorId) to get refresh token, posts to Emporia OAuth endpoint for new access token, calls credentialStore.write(vendorId, newCredentials), then retries the original request transparently.
target_output.data_model: EnergyReading: circuitId STRING, vendorId STRING (shelly/emporia/sense), timestamp Date UTC, kw_import FLOAT (positive = consuming), kw_export FLOAT optional (solar production), voltage FLOAT optional, frequency FLOAT optional, source VendorEnum. Circuit: id STRING, name STRING, vendorId STRING, panel STRING optional, phases 1|2|3, ratedAmps INT optional. AggregationResult: circuitId STRING, windowStart Date, windowEnd Date, granularity minute|hour|day, total_kwh FLOAT, min_kw FLOAT, max_kw FLOAT, avg_kw FLOAT, sample_count INT. VendorCredentials: vendorId STRING, accessToken STRING, refreshToken STRING optional, expiresAt Date optional, scope STRING optional.
target_output.api_design: npm package API (no HTTP endpoints). import { ShellyAdapter } from '@energy-sdk/shelly'; import { EmporiaAdapter } from '@energy-sdk/emporia'; import { AggregationEngine } from '@energy-sdk/core'. const adapter = new ShellyAdapter({ credentialStore, devices }). const circuits = await adapter.getCircuits(). for await (const reading of adapter.getReadings(circuitId)) { ... }. const hourly = await adapter.getAggregated(circuitId, { granularity: 'hour', start, end }). AggregationEngine.aggregate(iterable, 'day') -> Promise<AggregationResult[]>. All methods typed with TypeScript generics; full JSDoc on public API.
target_output.security_and_compliance: Credential protection: CredentialStore interface requires callers to manage storage; SDK never persists credentials in module scope or emits them in logs. OAuth token values are masked in EnergySDKError messages: only token expiry time and vendor name are included. HTTPS enforced with TLS certificate validation enabled on all vendor API calls; no option to disable. GDPR security guide in docs/security.md documents that EnergyReading objects are personal data when associated with a residential address, recommending data minimization, retention limits, and processor agreements for EU deployments. MIT license; no telemetry.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: Monorepo managed with Bun workspaces. Each package has independent package.json with peerDependencies on @energy-sdk/core. Bun build outputs ESM-only to dist/ with source maps and .d.ts files from tsc. GitHub Actions CI matrix across Node 18, Node 20, Bun 1.x: bun install, bun test, bun run build, Codecov upload. Changeset bot enforces a changeset file on every PR. Release workflow: changeset version bumps package versions, npm publish @energy-sdk/core then adapters with --provenance, creates GitHub Release with changelog. bundlemon action checks gzipped core bundle does not exceed 15 KB.
target_output.observability: Codecov with 90% line and branch coverage gate blocking PR merge. bundlemon reports gzipped bundle size delta per PR with fail-on-increase flag for core package. msw-based integration mock tests run on every CI push. Vendor sandbox integration tests tagged @integration and scheduled weekly to detect vendor API drift. npm package size reported in GitHub Actions job summary on publish.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Zero hosting cost: GitHub Actions free tier for open-source. npm publish is free. bundlemon SaaS free tier covers bundle monitoring. Vendor rate limiting built into adapters so SDK consumers are protected from accidental API quota exhaustion regardless of polling frequency requested.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 v1.0 (8 weeks): @energy-sdk/core with EnergyMonitorAdapter interface, EnergyReading type, AggregationEngine, CredentialStore interface, error hierarchy; ShellyAdapter and EmporiaAdapter with zod validation; bun test suite with msw mocks; Codecov at 90%; npm publish workflow with changeset. Phase 2 v1.1 (4 weeks): SenseAdapter via WebSocket AsyncIterable, streaming aggregation mode, RateLimitError with retry_after, GDPR security guide. Phase 3 v1.2 (3 weeks): @energy-sdk/react hooks package with useEnergyReadings and useAggregation hooks; CLI tool @energy-sdk/cli for ad-hoc queries.
target_output.development_guidelines: TypeScript strict mode with exactOptionalPropertyTypes enabled. ESLint with @typescript-eslint recommended rules. bun test for all unit tests; no jest. Named ESM exports only; no default exports to maintain tree-shakeability. Every public method must have a JSDoc comment with @param and @returns. Vendor response zod schemas must be defined in a separate schemas.ts file per adapter for easy update when vendor APIs change.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Emporia GraphQL schema changes without deprecation notice, breaking the adapter silently. Mitigation: zod strict schema validation throws at normalization time rather than returning corrupted EnergyReading objects; CI integration tests run weekly. Risk: Sense WebSocket protocol is undocumented and may change on app update. Mitigation: SenseAdapter v1.1 marked as experimental in README with explicit stability disclaimer. Tradeoff: ESM-only output versus dual CJS/ESM builds; ESM-only accepted given Node 18+ and Bun 1.x targets both support native ESM.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Selected AsyncIterable over EventEmitter for streaming readings because it composes with for-await-of and AggregationEngine.aggregate without callback indirection. Confirmed Bun as build toolchain over Vite since library mode Vite adds unnecessary complexity for a pure TypeScript package. Zod chosen over io-ts for vendor response validation due to better TypeScript inference ergonomics.

metadata.notes: First-pass moderate-quality TypeScript SDK for unified home energy monitor vendor API abstraction with normalization and aggregation.

profile.domain: Power Grid Acoustic Fault Detection AI

project_goal.value: Real-time AI system processing live 48 kHz audio from acoustic emission sensors on high-voltage power line infrastructure to detect arcing faults, insulator cracking, and conductor galloping before they cause outages
project_goal.rationale: Grid operators need sub-minute fault detection to dispatch maintenance before cascading failures

target_users.value: Grid operations center engineers monitoring transmission line health in real time and field maintenance dispatch coordinators
target_users.rationale: Primary operational roles for power grid fault management

project_class.rationale: Core value is an ML inference pipeline processing real-time audio streams for fault classification

capabilities.value: backend, data, auth, ai_llm, realtime, analytics, devops
capabilities.rationale: Requires real-time stream processing, ML inference, alerting, analytics, and infrastructure automation

complexity_level.rationale: Thousands of sensors each streaming 48 kHz audio requiring low-latency feature extraction and parallel inference

risk_level.rationale: Critical infrastructure; false negatives risk grid outages; false positives cause unnecessary maintenance dispatch

data_sensitivity.rationale: Grid topology, sensor placement, and fault patterns are operationally sensitive and subject to NERC CIP

external_exposure.rationale: Grid operations console accessible only to authenticated utility company personnel

access_model.value: Azure AD RBAC with grid_operator, maintenance_dispatcher, and readonly_executive roles; MFA enforced for all roles
access_model.rationale: Critical infrastructure requires multi-factor authentication for all access

feature_scope.value: Acoustic sensor audio ingestion, 500ms window spectral feature extraction, fault classification model inference (arcing/insulator_crack/conductor_galloping/normal), confidence-thresholded alert dispatch, operator alert queue, grid segment map with fault overlay, annotation and feedback loop for model retraining, model performance dashboard
feature_scope.rationale: Full operational feature set for power grid acoustic monitoring

mvp_scope.value: Audio ingestion pipeline, arcing fault detection model, alert dispatch to on-call engineers, GPS-located fault events on segment map
mvp_scope.rationale: Arcing faults cause most emergency outages; MVP focuses on the highest-priority event type

security_baseline.value: TLS 1.3 for all sensor-to-ingestion traffic; Azure AD MFA for all personnel access; network isolation: sensors on a dedicated VLAN with no internet egress; audit logging of all model inference results and operator actions per NERC CIP-007
security_baseline.rationale: NERC CIP critical infrastructure protection standards mandate network segmentation and audit logging

frontend_stack.value: none - no frontend specialist; operational console is out of scope for this architecture
frontend_stack.rationale: ai_system project class; frontend is a separate initiative

backend_stack.value: Python 3.12 with FastAPI for inference API; Kafka Streams for audio frame processing; ONNX Runtime for model inference
backend_stack.rationale: ONNX Runtime provides hardware-accelerated inference on GPU nodes; Kafka buffers audio frames for parallel inference workers

data_platform.value: ClickHouse for fault event time-series analytics; Redis 7 for live sensor state and alert deduplication
data_platform.rationale: ClickHouse handles columnar time-series queries on billions of inference results; Redis provides sub-millisecond alert dedup lookups

hosting_target.value: AKS (Azure Kubernetes Service) in Azure Government Cloud regions for NERC CIP compliance
hosting_target.rationale: Azure Government Cloud meets NERC CIP physical and logical isolation requirements for critical infrastructure

privacy_retention_policy.value: Raw audio streams retained 72 hours then deleted; fault_detections and event_classifications retained 7 years per NERC CIP audit requirements; model inference logs retained 1 year
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: NERC CIP-009 requires 7-year retention of security and operational event records

future_scope.value: Thermal imaging sensor fusion, predictive maintenance scheduling integration, multi-utility SaaS offering
future_scope.rationale: Multi-modal sensor fusion improves detection accuracy; SaaS is a monetization option post-pilot

constraints.value: Fault alert must be dispatched within 45 seconds of sensor event; inference pipeline must sustain 2,000 concurrent audio streams; no public cloud API calls for LLM inference (on-premises GPU cluster required for NERC CIP isolation)
constraints.rationale: 45-second SLA is the grid operator requirement; NERC CIP prohibits sending operational data to public cloud APIs

observability_baseline.value: Grafana dashboards with Prometheus metrics; alerts via PagerDuty for pipeline lag, inference error rate, and missed alert SLA; ClickHouse for long-term fault analytics
observability_baseline.rationale: Grid operations requires 24/7 monitoring with on-call escalation

execution_preference.value: GitOps with ArgoCD for AKS deployments; 2-week sprint cycles; canary model deployments with automated rollback on precision degradation
execution_preference.rationale: GitOps enables auditable infrastructure changes required by NERC CIP change management

compliance_context.value: NERC CIP-007 systems security management, CIP-009 recovery plans, CIP-011 information protection; SOC 2 Type II for SaaS platform layer; all model inference results and operator actions must be logged for 7-year audit trail
compliance_context.rationale: High risk_level and confidential data_sensitivity on critical infrastructure mandate NERC CIP compliance

llm_integration.value: On-premises fine-tuned acoustic fault classification model: ONNX-exported transformer encoder trained on labeled acoustic emission datasets (arcing, insulator_crack, conductor_galloping, normal); no public LLM API calls; model artifacts stored in Azure Blob Storage private to the AKS cluster
llm_integration.rationale: NERC CIP isolation requirement prohibits sending sensor data to public APIs; on-premises ONNX inference satisfies this constraint

project.goal: Real-time acoustic fault detection for high-voltage transmission lines
project.target_users: Grid operations engineers and maintenance dispatchers
project.timeline: MVP in 16 weeks, full fault class coverage in 24 weeks

backend.framework: FastAPI for inference API; Kafka Streams consumer for audio processing
backend.inference: ONNX Runtime with GPU acceleration on A100 nodes

security.network: Sensor VLAN isolated from internet; TLS 1.3 sensor-to-ingestion

data.time_series: ClickHouse for fault events and inference logs
data.cache: Redis 7 for live sensor state and alert dedup
data.audio: Raw audio on Azure Blob with 72h TTL

devops.environments: dev, staging, production in Azure Government Cloud

reasoner_reviews.product: The 45-second alert SLA is the hardest constraint. With 500ms inference windows, the pipeline has budget for approximately 30 inference windows before the SLA expires, meaning queuing delay must be near zero. False positive rate needs careful tuning: unnecessary maintenance dispatch on a transmission line requires crew mobilization which is expensive and dangerous.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: ONNX Runtime TensorRT execution provider on A100 gives roughly 2ms inference latency per 500ms window batch. With 2,000 concurrent streams, the inference service needs 4 GPU worker pods each handling 500 streams if batched at 64 windows per inference call. The Kafka consumer group must use range assignment to ensure each worker pod owns a contiguous partition range, avoiding cross-pod audio frame ordering issues.
reasoner_reviews.security: NERC CIP-011 requires that acoustic sensor data, which reveals grid topology, is classified as Protected Cyber Asset information. All ClickHouse data must be encrypted at rest with Azure-managed keys. The audit log must capture every inference result with sensor_id, fault_class, confidence, and operator action taken. Azure AD Conditional Access policies must enforce MFA plus compliant-device requirement for grid operators.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: Round 2 plan left the model retraining feedback loop undefined. Operator annotations feed a fine-tuning cycle but the labeling UI, training job schedule, and model promotion gate were not specified. Also the audio retention deletion job was mentioned but not the AKS CronJob that executes it.
reasoner_reviews.critic: The previous plan improved inference throughput spec but still has gaps: the model promotion gate does not specify a minimum precision threshold for each fault class before production rollout, and the audio deletion CronJob schedule was mentioned in prose but not specified as a Kubernetes manifest annotation.

backend.service_design: Four Python microservices on AKS: audio-ingestor (Kafka producer consuming MQTT from sensor VLAN), feature-extractor (Kafka consumer running librosa spectral feature extraction on 500ms windows, publishing to features topic), inference-engine (Kafka consumer, ONNX Runtime GPU inference, publishes to detections topic), alert-dispatcher (Kafka consumer, evaluates confidence threshold, deduplicates via Redis, publishes to PagerDuty and ClickHouse).
backend.llm_integration_design: ONNX-exported transformer encoder fine-tuned on 40,000 labeled acoustic emission samples across four fault classes. Served via ONNX Runtime with TensorRT execution provider on A100 nodes. Model artifacts stored in Azure Blob Storage private container. Inference batched at 64 windows per call for GPU efficiency. Model version tracked in Redis sensor_state for rollback telemetry.
backend.background_jobs: Weekly model fine-tuning job: AKS CronJob pulls operator-annotated samples from ClickHouse training_samples table, runs fine-tuning script on GPU node pool, evaluates against held-out test set; if precision per fault class exceeds thresholds (arcing >0.92, insulator_crack >0.88, conductor_galloping >0.85), promotes artifact to Blob Storage production path via ArgoCD image update. Audio deletion CronJob: runs nightly at 01:00 UTC, deletes Azure Blob audio objects with last_modified older than 72 hours.
backend.scaling_notes: Inference engine auto-scales from 2 to 8 GPU pods based on Kafka consumer lag on features topic. Feature extractor scales from 4 to 16 CPU pods on audio frames topic lag > 1000.

security.auth_design: Azure AD RBAC tenant with NERC CIP role assignments. Conditional Access policy requires MFA plus Microsoft Compliant Device for all grid_operator and maintenance_dispatcher sign-ins. Azure AD tokens validated by FastAPI middleware on inference API.
security.authorization_model: grid_operator: read all fault_detections and event_classifications, write operator annotations. maintenance_dispatcher: read fault_detections, write dispatch confirmations. readonly_executive: read aggregated analytics views only.
security.audit_and_logging_controls: Every inference result written to ClickHouse inference_audit table with sensor_id, fault_class, confidence_score, model_version, inferred_at. Operator actions written to operator_audit table with user_id, action_type, fault_detection_id, timestamp. Azure Monitor captures all AKS control plane events. Retained 7 years per NERC CIP-009.
security.security_risks: Risk: compromised sensor feeding adversarial audio to manipulate inference results. Mitigation: sensor authentication via client TLS certificates; anomalous audio frames flagged if spectral energy outside physical plausibility bounds.

data.entities: sensor_units (id, line_segment_id, gps_lat, gps_lng, install_date, status). audio_streams (id, sensor_id, stream_start, blob_path, sample_rate_hz, duration_ms - Azure Blob reference). fault_detections (id, sensor_id, window_start, fault_class ENUM arcing/insulator_crack/conductor_galloping/normal, confidence FLOAT, model_version, alert_dispatched BOOL, created_at). event_classifications (id, fault_detection_id, operator_annotation ENUM confirmed/false_positive/upgraded, annotated_by, annotated_at). power_line_segments (id, name, voltage_kv, start_substation, end_substation, length_km, geojson_path). maintenance_dispatches (id, fault_detection_id, crew_id, dispatched_at, resolved_at).
data.storage_design: ClickHouse: fault_detections and event_classifications in a MergeTree table partitioned by toYYYYMM(created_at) with TTL for inference_audit at 1 year; operator_audit at 7 years. Redis HASH per sensor_id for live state (last_fault_class, last_confidence, last_inference_at). Azure Blob Storage for raw audio with 72-hour lifecycle policy.
data.analytics_events: Prometheus metrics: inference_pipeline_lag_seconds, fault_detection_rate_per_minute per fault_class, alert_dispatch_latency_seconds. ClickHouse queries: precision and recall per fault_class per week, false_positive_rate by segment, mean time to dispatch by fault severity.

devops.deployment_topology: AKS cluster in Azure Government us-gov-virginia: node pools - system (3 nodes), cpu-workers (4-16 nodes auto-scale for ingestors and feature extractors), gpu-workers (2-8 nodes Standard_NC24ads_A100_v4 for inference engine). ArgoCD GitOps sync from infrastructure repo.
devops.ci_cd_design: Azure DevOps pipeline: pytest with 80% coverage gate, Docker build, push Azure Container Registry, update image tag in Helm values file in infrastructure repo, ArgoCD auto-syncs. Model promotion: separate pipeline triggered by fine-tuning CronJob completion, runs evaluation script, updates model_version ConfigMap in production namespace if thresholds pass.
devops.rollback_strategy: ArgoCD rollback to previous Helm revision on inference error rate > 5% in first 10 minutes post-deploy. Model rollback: update model_version ConfigMap to previous artifact path; inference pods reload model on config watch.
devops.devops_risks: GPU node pool scale-up latency (3-5 minutes) may delay inference during burst. Mitigation: minimum 2 GPU nodes always running; scale-up trigger at Kafka lag > 500 rather than waiting for full saturation.
